16
17/**
18 * This MPM tries to fix the 'keep alive problem' in HTTP.
19 *
20 * After a client completes the first request, the client can keep the
21 * connection open to send more requests with the same socket.  This can save
22 * significant overhead in creating TCP connections.  However, the major
23 * disadvantage is that Apache traditionally keeps an entire child
24 * process/thread waiting for data from the client.  To solve this problem,
25 * this MPM has a dedicated thread for handling both the Listening sockets,
26 * and all sockets that are in a Keep Alive status.
27 *
28 * The MPM assumes the underlying apr_pollset implementation is somewhat
29 * threadsafe.  This currently is only compatible with KQueue and EPoll.  This
30 * enables the MPM to avoid extra high level locking or having to wake up the
31 * listener thread when a keep-alive socket needs to be sent to it.
32 *
33 * This MPM does not perform well on older platforms that do not have very good
34 * threading, like Linux with a 2.4 kernel, but this does not matter, since we
35 * require EPoll or KQueue.
36 *
37 * For FreeBSD, use 5.3.  It is possible to run this MPM on FreeBSD 5.2.1, if
38 * you use libkse (see `man libmap.conf`).
39 *
40 * For NetBSD, use at least 2.0.
41 *
42 * For Linux, you should use a 2.6 kernel, and make sure your glibc has epoll
43 * support compiled in.
44 *
45 */ view japanese
46
47#include "apr.h"

75#endif

78#error The Event MPM requires APR threads, but they are unavailable.
79#endif

101
102
103/* Limit on the total --- clients will be locked out if more servers than
104 * this are needed.  It is intended solely to keep the server from crashing
105 * when things get out of hand.
106 *
107 * We keep a hard maximum number of servers, for two reasons --- first off,
108 * in case something goes seriously wrong, we want to stop the fork bomb
109 * short of actually crashing the machine we're running on by filling some
110 * kernel table.  Secondly, it keeps the size of the scoreboard file small
111 * enough that we can read the whole thing without worrying too much about
112 * the overhead.
113 */ view japanese
114#ifndef DEFAULT_SERVER_LIMIT
115#define DEFAULT_SERVER_LIMIT 16
116#endif
117
118/* Admin can't tune ServerLimit beyond MAX_SERVER_LIMIT.  We want
119 * some sort of compile-time limit to help catch typos.
120 */ view japanese
121#ifndef MAX_SERVER_LIMIT
122#define MAX_SERVER_LIMIT 20000
123#endif
124
125/* Limit on the threads per process.  Clients will be locked out if more than
126 * this are needed.
127 *
128 * We keep this for one reason it keeps the size of the scoreboard file small
129 * enough that we can read the whole thing without worrying too much about
130 * the overhead.
131 */ view japanese
132#ifndef DEFAULT_THREAD_LIMIT
133#define DEFAULT_THREAD_LIMIT 64
134#endif
135
136/* Admin can't tune ThreadLimit beyond MAX_THREAD_LIMIT.  We want
137 * some sort of compile-time limit to help catch typos.
138 */ view japanese
139#ifndef MAX_THREAD_LIMIT
140#define MAX_THREAD_LIMIT 100000
141#endif
142
143#define MPM_CHILD_PID(i) (ap_scoreboard_image->parent[i].pid)
144
145#if !APR_VERSION_AT_LEAST(1,4,0)
146#define apr_time_from_msec(x) (x * 1000)
147#endif
148
149#define CONN_STATE_IS_LINGERING_CLOSE(s) ((s) >= CONN_STATE_LINGER && \
150                                          (s) <= CONN_STATE_LINGER_SHORT)
151#ifndef MAX_SECS_TO_LINGER
152#define MAX_SECS_TO_LINGER 30
153#endif
154#define SECONDS_TO_LINGER  2
155
156/*
157 * Actual definitions of config globals
158 */ view japanese
159
160#ifndef DEFAULT_WORKER_FACTOR
161#define DEFAULT_WORKER_FACTOR 2
162#endif
163#define WORKER_FACTOR_SCALE   16  /* scale factor to allow fractional values */ view japanese
164static unsigned int worker_factor = DEFAULT_WORKER_FACTOR * WORKER_FACTOR_SCALE;
165    /* AsyncRequestWorkerFactor * 16 */ view japanese
166
167static int threads_per_child = 0;           /* ThreadsPerChild */ view japanese
168static int ap_daemons_to_start = 0;         /* StartServers */ view japanese
169static int min_spare_threads = 0;           /* MinSpareThreads */ view japanese
170static int max_spare_threads = 0;           /* MaxSpareThreads */ view japanese
171static int active_daemons_limit = 0;        /* MaxRequestWorkers / ThreadsPerChild */ view japanese
172static int max_workers = 0;                 /* MaxRequestWorkers */ view japanese
173static int server_limit = 0;                /* ServerLimit */ view japanese
174static int thread_limit = 0;                /* ThreadLimit */ view japanese
175static int had_healthy_child = 0;
176static volatile int dying = 0;
177static volatile int workers_may_exit = 0;
178static volatile int start_thread_may_exit = 0;
179static volatile int listener_may_exit = 0;
180static int listener_is_wakeable = 0;        /* Pollset supports APR_POLLSET_WAKEABLE */ view japanese
181static int num_listensocks = 0;
182static apr_int32_t conns_this_child;        /* MaxConnectionsPerChild, only access
183                                               in listener thread */ view japanese
184static apr_uint32_t connection_count = 0;   /* Number of open connections */ view japanese
185static apr_uint32_t lingering_count = 0;    /* Number of connections in lingering close */ view japanese
186static apr_uint32_t suspended_count = 0;    /* Number of suspended connections */ view japanese
187static apr_uint32_t clogged_count = 0;      /* Number of threads processing ssl conns */ view japanese
188static apr_uint32_t threads_shutdown = 0;   /* Number of threads that have shutdown
189                                               early during graceful termination */ view japanese
190static int resource_shortage = 0;
191static fd_queue_t *worker_queue;
192static fd_queue_info_t *worker_queue_info;
193
194static apr_thread_mutex_t *timeout_mutex;
195
196module AP_MODULE_DECLARE_DATA mpm_event_module;
197
198/* forward declare */ view japanese
199struct event_srv_cfg_s;
200typedef struct event_srv_cfg_s event_srv_cfg;
201
202static apr_pollfd_t *listener_pollfd;
203
204/*
205 * The pollset for sockets that are in any of the timeout queues. Currently
206 * we use the timeout_mutex to make sure that connections are added/removed
207 * atomically to/from both event_pollset and a timeout queue. Otherwise
208 * some confusion can happen under high load if timeout queues and pollset
209 * get out of sync.
210 * XXX: It should be possible to make the lock unnecessary in many or even all
211 * XXX: cases.
212 */ view japanese
213static apr_pollset_t *event_pollset;
214
215typedef struct event_conn_state_t event_conn_state_t;
216
217/*
218 * The chain of connections to be shutdown by a worker thread (deferred),
219 * linked list updated atomically.
220 */ view japanese
221static event_conn_state_t *volatile defer_linger_chain;
222
223struct event_conn_state_t {
224    /** APR_RING of expiration timeouts */ view japanese
225    APR_RING_ENTRY(event_conn_state_t) timeout_list;
226    /** the time when the entry was queued */ view japanese
227    apr_time_t queue_timestamp;
228    /** connection record this struct refers to */ view japanese
229    conn_rec *c;
230    /** request record (if any) this struct refers to */ view japanese
231    request_rec *r;
232    /** server config this struct refers to */ view japanese
233    event_srv_cfg *sc;
234    /** scoreboard handle for the conn_rec */ view japanese
235    ap_sb_handle_t *sbh;
236    /** is the current conn_rec suspended?  (disassociated with
237     * a particular MPM thread; for suspend_/resume_connection
238     * hooks)
239     */ view japanese
240    int suspended;
241    /** memory pool to allocate from */ view japanese
242    apr_pool_t *p;
243    /** bucket allocator */ view japanese
244    apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc;
245    /** poll file descriptor information */ view japanese
246    apr_pollfd_t pfd;
247    /** public parts of the connection state */ view japanese
248    conn_state_t pub;
249    /** chaining in defer_linger_chain */ view japanese
250    struct event_conn_state_t *chain;
251    unsigned int 
252        /** Is lingering close from defer_lingering_close()? */ view japanese
253        deferred_linger :1,
254        /** Has ap_start_lingering_close() been called? */ view japanese
255        linger_started  :1;
256};
257
258APR_RING_HEAD(timeout_head_t, event_conn_state_t);
259
260struct timeout_queue {
261    struct timeout_head_t head;
262    apr_interval_time_t timeout;
263    apr_uint32_t count;         /* for this queue */ view japanese
264    apr_uint32_t *total;        /* for all chained/related queues */ view japanese
265    struct timeout_queue *next; /* chaining */ view japanese
266};
267/*
268 * Several timeout queues that use different timeouts, so that we always can
269 * simply append to the end.
270 *   waitio_q           uses vhost's TimeOut
271 *   write_completion_q uses vhost's TimeOut
272 *   keepalive_q        uses vhost's KeepAliveTimeOut
273 *   linger_q           uses MAX_SECS_TO_LINGER
274 *   short_linger_q     uses SECONDS_TO_LINGER
275 */ view japanese
276static struct timeout_queue *waitio_q,
277                            *write_completion_q,
278                            *keepalive_q,
279                            *linger_q,
280                            *short_linger_q;
281static volatile apr_time_t  queues_next_expiry;
282
283/* Prevent extra poll/wakeup calls for timeouts close in the future (queues
284 * have the granularity of a second anyway).
285 * XXX: Wouldn't 0.5s (instead of 0.1s) be "enough"?
286 */ view japanese
287#define TIMEOUT_FUDGE_FACTOR apr_time_from_msec(100)
288
289/*
290 * Macros for accessing struct timeout_queue.
291 * For TO_QUEUE_APPEND and TO_QUEUE_REMOVE, timeout_mutex must be held.
292 */ view japanese
293static void TO_QUEUE_APPEND(struct timeout_queue *q, event_conn_state_t *el)
294{
295    apr_time_t elem_expiry;
296    apr_time_t next_expiry;
297
298    APR_RING_INSERT_TAIL(&q->head, el, event_conn_state_t, timeout_list);
299    ++*q->total;
300    ++q->count;
301
302    /* Cheaply update the global queues_next_expiry with the one of the
303     * first entry of this queue (oldest) if it expires before.
304     */ view japanese
305    el = APR_RING_FIRST(&q->head);
306    elem_expiry = el->queue_timestamp + q->timeout;
307    next_expiry = queues_next_expiry;
308    if (!next_expiry || next_expiry > elem_expiry + TIMEOUT_FUDGE_FACTOR) {
309        queues_next_expiry = elem_expiry;
310        /* Unblock the poll()ing listener for it to update its timeout. */
311        if (listener_is_wakeable) {
312            apr_pollset_wakeup(event_pollset);
313        }
314    }
315}
316
317static void TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(struct timeout_queue *q, event_conn_state_t *el)
318{
319    APR_RING_REMOVE(el, timeout_list);
320    APR_RING_ELEM_INIT(el, timeout_list);
321    --*q->total;
322    --q->count;
323}
324
325static struct timeout_queue *TO_QUEUE_MAKE(apr_pool_t *p, apr_time_t t,
326                                           struct timeout_queue *ref)
327{
328    struct timeout_queue *q;
329                                           
330    q = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof *q);
331    APR_RING_INIT(&q->head, event_conn_state_t, timeout_list);
332    q->total = (ref) ? ref->total : apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof *q->total);
333    q->timeout = t;
334
335    return q;
336}
337
338#define TO_QUEUE_ELEM_INIT(el) \
339    APR_RING_ELEM_INIT((el), timeout_list)
340
341/* The structure used to pass unique initialization info to each thread */ view japanese
342typedef struct
343{
344    int pslot;  /* process slot */ view japanese
345    int tslot;  /* worker slot of the thread */ view japanese
346} proc_info;
347
348/* Structure used to pass information to the thread responsible for
349 * creating the rest of the threads.
350 */ view japanese
351typedef struct
352{
353    apr_thread_t **threads;
354    apr_thread_t *listener;
355    int child_num_arg;
356    apr_threadattr_t *threadattr;
357} thread_starter;
358
359typedef enum
360{
361    PT_CSD,
362    PT_ACCEPT
363} poll_type_e;
364
365typedef struct
366{
367    poll_type_e type;
368    void *baton;
369} listener_poll_type;
370
371/* data retained by event across load/unload of the module
372 * allocated on first call to pre-config hook; located on
373 * subsequent calls to pre-config hook
374 */ view japanese
375typedef struct event_retained_data {
376    ap_unixd_mpm_retained_data *mpm;
377
378    int first_server_limit;
379    int first_thread_limit;
380    int sick_child_detected;
381    int maxclients_reported;
382    int near_maxclients_reported;
383    /*
384     * The max child slot ever assigned, preserved across restarts.  Necessary
385     * to deal with MaxRequestWorkers changes across AP_SIG_GRACEFUL restarts.
386     * We use this value to optimize routines that have to scan the entire
387     * scoreboard.
388     */ view japanese
389    int max_daemon_used;
390
391    /*
392     * All running workers, active and shutting down, including those that
393     * may be left from before a graceful restart.
394     * Not kept up-to-date when shutdown is pending.
395     */ view japanese
396    int total_daemons;
397    /*
398     * Workers that still active, i.e. are not shutting down gracefully.
399     */ view japanese
400    int active_daemons;
401    /*
402     * idle_spawn_rate is the number of children that will be spawned on the
403     * next maintenance cycle if there aren't enough idle servers.  It is
404     * maintained per listeners bucket, doubled up to MAX_SPAWN_RATE, and
405     * reset only when a cycle goes by without the need to spawn.
406     */ view japanese
407    int *idle_spawn_rate;
408#ifndef MAX_SPAWN_RATE
409#define MAX_SPAWN_RATE        (32)
410#endif
411    int hold_off_on_exponential_spawning;
412} event_retained_data;
413static event_retained_data *retained;
414 
415typedef struct event_child_bucket {
416    ap_pod_t *pod;
417    ap_listen_rec *listeners;
418} event_child_bucket;
419static event_child_bucket *all_buckets, /* All listeners buckets */ view japanese
420                          *my_bucket;   /* Current child bucket */ view japanese
421
422struct event_srv_cfg_s {
423    struct timeout_queue *io_q,
424                         *wc_q,
425                         *ka_q;
426};
427
428#define ID_FROM_CHILD_THREAD(c, t)    ((c * thread_limit) + t)
429
430/* The event MPM respects a couple of runtime flags that can aid
431 * in debugging. Setting the -DNO_DETACH flag will prevent the root process
432 * from detaching from its controlling terminal. Additionally, setting
433 * the -DONE_PROCESS flag (which implies -DNO_DETACH) will get you the
434 * child_main loop running in the process which originally started up.
435 * This gives you a pretty nice debugging environment.  (You'll get a SIGHUP
436 * early in standalone_main; just continue through.  This is the server
437 * trying to kill off any child processes which it might have lying
438 * around --- Apache doesn't keep track of their pids, it just sends
439 * SIGHUP to the process group, ignoring it in the root process.
440 * Continue through and you'll be fine.).
441 */ view japanese
442
443static int one_process = 0;
444
445#ifdef DEBUG_SIGSTOP
446int raise_sigstop_flags;
447#endif
448
449static apr_pool_t *pconf;       /* Pool for config stuff */ view japanese
450static apr_pool_t *pchild;      /* Pool for httpd child stuff */ view japanese
451static apr_pool_t *pruntime;    /* Pool for MPM threads stuff */ view japanese
452
453static pid_t ap_my_pid;         /* Linux getpid() doesn't work except in main
454                                   thread. Use this instead */ view japanese
455static pid_t parent_pid;
456static apr_os_thread_t *listener_os_thread;
457
458static int ap_child_slot;       /* Current child process slot in scoreboard */ view japanese
459
460/* The LISTENER_SIGNAL signal will be sent from the main thread to the
461 * listener thread to wake it up for graceful termination (what a child
462 * process from an old generation does when the admin does "apachectl
463 * graceful").  This signal will be blocked in all threads of a child
464 * process except for the listener thread.
465 */ view japanese
466#define LISTENER_SIGNAL     SIGHUP
467
468/* An array of socket descriptors in use by each thread used to
469 * perform a non-graceful (forced) shutdown of the server.
470 */ view japanese
471static apr_socket_t **worker_sockets;
472
473static volatile apr_uint32_t listensocks_disabled;
474
475static void disable_listensocks(void)
476{
477    int i;
478    if (apr_atomic_cas32(&listensocks_disabled, 1, 0) != 0) {
479        return;
480    }
481    if (event_pollset) {
482        for (i = 0; i < num_listensocks; i++) {
483            apr_pollset_remove(event_pollset, &listener_pollfd[i]);
484        }
485    }
486    ap_scoreboard_image->parent[ap_child_slot].not_accepting = 1;
487}
488
489static void enable_listensocks(void)
490{
491    int i;
492    if (listener_may_exit
493            || apr_atomic_cas32(&listensocks_disabled, 0, 1) != 1) {
494        return;
495    }
496    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00457)
497                 "Accepting new connections again: "
498                 "%u active conns (%u lingering/%u clogged/%u suspended), "
499                 "%u idle workers",
500                 apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count),
501                 apr_atomic_read32(&lingering_count),
502                 apr_atomic_read32(&clogged_count),
503                 apr_atomic_read32(&suspended_count),
504                 ap_queue_info_num_idlers(worker_queue_info));
505    for (i = 0; i < num_listensocks; i++)
506        apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, &listener_pollfd[i]);
507    /*
508     * XXX: This is not yet optimal. If many workers suddenly become available,
509     * XXX: the parent may kill some processes off too soon.
510     */ view japanese
511    ap_scoreboard_image->parent[ap_child_slot].not_accepting = 0;
512}
513
514static APR_INLINE apr_uint32_t listeners_disabled(void)
515{
516    return apr_atomic_read32(&listensocks_disabled);
517}
518
519static APR_INLINE int connections_above_limit(int *busy)
520{
521    apr_uint32_t i_count = ap_queue_info_num_idlers(worker_queue_info);
522    if (i_count > 0) {
523        apr_uint32_t c_count = apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count);
524        apr_uint32_t l_count = apr_atomic_read32(&lingering_count);
525        if (c_count <= l_count
526                /* Off by 'listeners_disabled()' to avoid flip flop */ view japanese
527                || c_count - l_count < (apr_uint32_t)threads_per_child +
528                                       (i_count - listeners_disabled()) *
529                                       (worker_factor / WORKER_FACTOR_SCALE)) {
530            return 0;
531        }
532    }
533    else if (busy) {
534        *busy = 1;
535    }
536    return 1;
537}
538
539static APR_INLINE int should_enable_listensocks(void)
540{
541    return !dying && listeners_disabled() && !connections_above_limit(NULL);
542}
543
544static void close_socket_nonblocking_(apr_socket_t *csd,
545                                      const char *from, int line)
546{
547    apr_status_t rv;
548    apr_os_sock_t fd = -1;
549
550    /* close_worker_sockets() may have closed it already */ view japanese
551    rv = apr_os_sock_get(&fd, csd);
552    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, ap_server_conf,
553                "closing socket %i/%pp from %s:%i", (int)fd, csd, from, line);
554    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS && fd == -1) {
555        return;
556    }
557
558    apr_socket_timeout_set(csd, 0);
559    rv = apr_socket_close(csd);
560    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
561        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00468)
562                     "error closing socket");
563        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
564    }
565}
566#define close_socket_nonblocking(csd) \
567    close_socket_nonblocking_(csd, __FUNCTION__, __LINE__)
568
569static void close_worker_sockets(void)
570{
571    int i;
572    for (i = 0; i < threads_per_child; i++) {
573        apr_socket_t *csd = worker_sockets[i];
574        if (csd) {
575            worker_sockets[i] = NULL;
576            close_socket_nonblocking(csd);
577        }
578    }
579}
580
581static void wakeup_listener(void)
582{
583    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
584                 "wake up listener%s", listener_may_exit ? " again" : "");
585
586    listener_may_exit = 1;
587    disable_listensocks();
588
589    /* Unblock the listener if it's poll()ing */ view japanese
590    if (event_pollset && listener_is_wakeable) {
591        apr_pollset_wakeup(event_pollset);
592    }
593
594    /* unblock the listener if it's waiting for a worker */ view japanese
595    if (worker_queue_info) {
596        ap_queue_info_term(worker_queue_info);
597    }
598
599    if (!listener_os_thread) {
600        /* XXX there is an obscure path that this doesn't handle perfectly:
601         *     right after listener thread is created but before
602         *     listener_os_thread is set, the first worker thread hits an
603         *     error and starts graceful termination
604         */ view japanese
605        return;
606    }
607    /*
608     * we should just be able to "kill(ap_my_pid, LISTENER_SIGNAL)" on all
609     * platforms and wake up the listener thread since it is the only thread
610     * with SIGHUP unblocked, but that doesn't work on Linux
611     */ view japanese
612#ifdef HAVE_PTHREAD_KILL
613    pthread_kill(*listener_os_thread, LISTENER_SIGNAL);
614#else
615    kill(ap_my_pid, LISTENER_SIGNAL);
616#endif
617}
618
619#define ST_INIT              0
620#define ST_GRACEFUL          1
621#define ST_UNGRACEFUL        2
622
623static int terminate_mode = ST_INIT;
624
625static void signal_threads(int mode)
626{
627    if (terminate_mode >= mode) {
628        return;
629    }
630    terminate_mode = mode;
631    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STOPPING;
632
633    /* in case we weren't called from the listener thread, wake up the
634     * listener thread
635     */ view japanese
636    wakeup_listener();
637
638    /* for ungraceful termination, let the workers exit now;
639     * for graceful termination, the listener thread will notify the
640     * workers to exit once it has stopped accepting new connections
641     */ view japanese
642    if (mode == ST_UNGRACEFUL) {
643        workers_may_exit = 1;
644        ap_queue_interrupt_all(worker_queue);
645        close_worker_sockets(); /* forcefully kill all current connections */ view japanese
646    }
647
648    ap_run_child_stopping(pchild, mode == ST_GRACEFUL);
649}
650
651static int event_query(int query_code, int *result, apr_status_t *rv)
652{
653    *rv = APR_SUCCESS;
654    switch (query_code) {
655    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_DAEMON_USED:
656        *result = retained->max_daemon_used;
657        break;
658    case AP_MPMQ_IS_THREADED:
659        *result = AP_MPMQ_STATIC;
660        break;
661    case AP_MPMQ_IS_FORKED:
662        *result = AP_MPMQ_DYNAMIC;
663        break;
664    case AP_MPMQ_IS_ASYNC:
665        *result = 1;
666        break;
667    case AP_MPMQ_HARD_LIMIT_DAEMONS:
668        *result = server_limit;
669        break;
670    case AP_MPMQ_HARD_LIMIT_THREADS:
671        *result = thread_limit;
672        break;
673    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_THREADS:
674        *result = threads_per_child;
675        break;
676    case AP_MPMQ_MIN_SPARE_DAEMONS:
677        *result = 0;
678        break;
679    case AP_MPMQ_MIN_SPARE_THREADS:
680        *result = min_spare_threads;
681        break;
682    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_SPARE_DAEMONS:
683        *result = 0;
684        break;
685    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_SPARE_THREADS:
686        *result = max_spare_threads;
687        break;
688    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_REQUESTS_DAEMON:
689        *result = ap_max_requests_per_child;
690        break;
691    case AP_MPMQ_MAX_DAEMONS:
692        *result = active_daemons_limit;
693        break;
694    case AP_MPMQ_MPM_STATE:
695        *result = retained->mpm->mpm_state;
696        break;
697    case AP_MPMQ_GENERATION:
698        *result = retained->mpm->my_generation;
699        break;
700    case AP_MPMQ_CAN_WAITIO:
701        *result = 1;
702        break;
703    default:
704        *rv = APR_ENOTIMPL;
705        break;
706    }
707    return OK;
708}
709
710static void event_note_child_stopped(int slot, pid_t pid, ap_generation_t gen)
711{
712    if (slot != -1) { /* child had a scoreboard slot? */ view japanese
713        process_score *ps = &ap_scoreboard_image->parent[slot];
714        int i;
715
716        pid = ps->pid;
717        gen = ps->generation;
718        for (i = 0; i < threads_per_child; i++) {
719            ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(slot, i, SERVER_DEAD, NULL);
720        }
721        ap_run_child_status(ap_server_conf, pid, gen, slot, MPM_CHILD_EXITED);
722        if (ps->quiescing != 2) { /* vs perform_idle_server_maintenance() */ view japanese
723            retained->active_daemons--;
724        }
725        retained->total_daemons--;
726        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
727                     "Child %d stopped: pid %d, gen %d, "
728                     "active %d/%d, total %d/%d/%d, quiescing %d",
729                     slot, (int)pid, (int)gen,
730                     retained->active_daemons, active_daemons_limit,
731                     retained->total_daemons, retained->max_daemon_used,
732                     server_limit, ps->quiescing);
733        ps->not_accepting = 0;
734        ps->quiescing = 0;
735        ps->pid = 0;
736    }
737    else {
738        ap_run_child_status(ap_server_conf, pid, gen, -1, MPM_CHILD_EXITED);
739    }
740}
741
742static void event_note_child_started(int slot, pid_t pid)
743{
744    ap_generation_t gen = retained->mpm->my_generation;
745
746    retained->total_daemons++;
747    retained->active_daemons++;
748    ap_scoreboard_image->parent[slot].pid = pid;
749    ap_scoreboard_image->parent[slot].generation = gen;
750    ap_run_child_status(ap_server_conf, pid, gen, slot, MPM_CHILD_STARTED);
751    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
752                 "Child %d started: pid %d, gen %d, "
753                 "active %d/%d, total %d/%d/%d",
754                 slot, (int)pid, (int)gen,
755                 retained->active_daemons, active_daemons_limit,
756                 retained->total_daemons, retained->max_daemon_used,
757                 server_limit);
758}
759
760static const char *event_get_name(void)
761{
762    return "event";
763}
764
765/* a clean exit from a child with proper cleanup */ view japanese
766static void clean_child_exit(int code) __attribute__ ((noreturn));
767static void clean_child_exit(int code)
768{
769    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STOPPING;
770    if (terminate_mode == ST_INIT) {
771        ap_run_child_stopping(pchild, 0);
772    }
773
774    if (pchild) {
775        apr_pool_destroy(pchild);
776    }
777
778    if (one_process) {
779        event_note_child_stopped(/* slot */ 0, 0, 0); view japanese
780    }
781
782    exit(code);
783}
784
785static void just_die(int sig)
786{
787    clean_child_exit(0);
788}
789
790/*****************************************************************
791 * Connection structures and accounting...
792 */ view japanese
793
794static int child_fatal;
795
796static apr_status_t decrement_connection_count(void *cs_)
797{
798    int is_last_connection;
799    event_conn_state_t *cs = cs_;
800    ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, cs->c,
801                  "cleanup connection from state %i", (int)cs->pub.state);
802    switch (cs->pub.state) {
803        case CONN_STATE_LINGER:
804        case CONN_STATE_LINGER_NORMAL:
805        case CONN_STATE_LINGER_SHORT:
806            apr_atomic_dec32(&lingering_count);
807            break;
808        case CONN_STATE_SUSPENDED:
809            apr_atomic_dec32(&suspended_count);
810            break;
811        default:
812            break;
813    }
814    /* Unblock the listener if it's waiting for connection_count = 0,
815     * or if the listening sockets were disabled due to limits and can
816     * now accept new connections.
817     */ view japanese
818    is_last_connection = !apr_atomic_dec32(&connection_count);
819    if (listener_is_wakeable
820            && ((is_last_connection && listener_may_exit)
821                || should_enable_listensocks())) {
822        apr_pollset_wakeup(event_pollset);
823    }
824    if (dying) {
825        /* Help worker_thread_should_exit_early() */ view japanese
826        ap_queue_interrupt_one(worker_queue);
827    }
828    return APR_SUCCESS;
829}
830
831static void notify_suspend(event_conn_state_t *cs)
832{
833    ap_run_suspend_connection(cs->c, cs->r);
834    cs->c->sbh = NULL;
835    cs->suspended = 1;
836}
837
838static void notify_resume(event_conn_state_t *cs, int cleanup)
839{
840    cs->suspended = 0;
841    cs->c->sbh = cleanup ? NULL : cs->sbh;
842    ap_run_resume_connection(cs->c, cs->r);
843}
844
845/*
846 * Defer flush and close of the connection by adding it to defer_linger_chain,
847 * for a worker to grab it and do the job (should that be blocking).
848 * Pre-condition: nonblocking, can be called from anywhere provided cs is not
849 *                in any timeout queue or in the pollset.
850 */ view japanese
851static int defer_lingering_close(event_conn_state_t *cs)
852{
853    ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, cs->c,
854                  "deferring close from state %i", (int)cs->pub.state);
855
856    /* The connection is not shutdown() yet strictly speaking, but it's not
857     * in any queue nor handled by a worker either (will be very soon), so
858     * to account for it somewhere we bump lingering_count now (and set
859     * deferred_linger for process_lingering_close() to know).
860     */ view japanese
861    cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
862    apr_atomic_inc32(&lingering_count);
863    cs->deferred_linger = 1;
864    for (;;) {
865        event_conn_state_t *chain = cs->chain = defer_linger_chain;
866        if (apr_atomic_casptr((void *)&defer_linger_chain, cs,
867                              chain) != chain) {
868            /* Race lost, try again */
869            continue;
870        }
871        return 1;
872    }
873}
874
875/* Close the connection and release its resources (ptrans), either because an
876 * unrecoverable error occured (queues or pollset add/remove) or more usually
877 * if lingering close timed out.
878 * Pre-condition: nonblocking, can be called from anywhere provided cs is not
879 *                in any timeout queue or in the pollset.
880 */ view japanese
881static void close_connection(event_conn_state_t *cs)
882{
883    ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, cs->c,
884                  "closing connection from state %i", (int)cs->pub.state);
885
886    close_socket_nonblocking(cs->pfd.desc.s);
887    ap_queue_info_push_pool(worker_queue_info, cs->p);
888}
889
890/* Shutdown the connection in case of timeout, error or resources shortage.
891 * This starts short lingering close if not already there, or directly closes
892 * the connection otherwise.
893 * Pre-condition: nonblocking, can be called from anywhere provided cs is not
894 *                in any timeout queue or in the pollset.
895 */ view japanese
896static int shutdown_connection(event_conn_state_t *cs)
897{
898    if (!CONN_STATE_IS_LINGERING_CLOSE(cs->pub.state)) {
899        apr_table_setn(cs->c->notes, "short-lingering-close", "1");
900        defer_lingering_close(cs);
901    }
902    else {
903        close_connection(cs);
904    }
905    return 1;
906}
907
908/*
909 * This runs before any non-MPM cleanup code on the connection;
910 * if the connection is currently suspended as far as modules
911 * know, provide notification of resumption.
912 */ view japanese
913static apr_status_t ptrans_pre_cleanup(void *dummy)
914{
915    event_conn_state_t *cs = dummy;
916
917    if (cs->suspended) {
918        notify_resume(cs, 1);
919    }
920    return APR_SUCCESS;
921}
922
923/*
924 * event_pre_read_request() and event_request_cleanup() track the
925 * current r for a given connection.
926 */ view japanese
927static apr_status_t event_request_cleanup(void *dummy)
928{
929    conn_rec *c = dummy;
930    event_conn_state_t *cs = ap_get_module_config(c->conn_config,
931                                                  &mpm_event_module);
932
933    cs->r = NULL;
934    return APR_SUCCESS;
935}
936
937static void event_pre_read_request(request_rec *r, conn_rec *c)
938{
939    event_conn_state_t *cs = ap_get_module_config(c->conn_config,
940                                                  &mpm_event_module);
941
942    cs->r = r;
943    cs->sc = ap_get_module_config(ap_server_conf->module_config,
944                                  &mpm_event_module);
945    apr_pool_cleanup_register(r->pool, c, event_request_cleanup,
946                              apr_pool_cleanup_null);
947}
948
949/*
950 * event_post_read_request() tracks the current server config for a
951 * given request.
952 */ view japanese
953static int event_post_read_request(request_rec *r)
954{
955    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
956    event_conn_state_t *cs = ap_get_module_config(c->conn_config,
957                                                  &mpm_event_module);
958
959    /* To preserve legacy behaviour (consistent with other MPMs), use
960     * the keepalive timeout from the base server (first on this IP:port)
961     * when none is explicitly configured on this server.
962     */ view japanese
963    if (r->server->keep_alive_timeout_set) {
964        cs->sc = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
965                                      &mpm_event_module);
966    }
967    else {
968        cs->sc = ap_get_module_config(c->base_server->module_config,
969                                      &mpm_event_module);
970    }
971    return OK;
972}
973
974/* Forward declare */ view japanese
975static void process_lingering_close(event_conn_state_t *cs);
976
977static void update_reqevents_from_sense(event_conn_state_t *cs,
978                                        int default_sense)
979{
980    int sense = default_sense;
981
982    if (cs->pub.sense != CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT) {
983        sense = cs->pub.sense;
984
985        /* Reset to default for the next round */ view japanese
986        cs->pub.sense = CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT;
987    }
988
989    if (sense == CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ) {
990        cs->pfd.reqevents = APR_POLLIN | APR_POLLHUP;
991    }
992    else {
993        cs->pfd.reqevents = APR_POLLOUT;
994    }
995    /* POLLERR is usually returned event only, but some pollset
996     * backends may require it in reqevents to do the right thing,
997     * so it shouldn't hurt (ignored otherwise).
998     */ view japanese
999    cs->pfd.reqevents |= APR_POLLERR;
1000}
1001
1002/*
1003 * process one connection in the worker
1004 */ view japanese
1005static void process_socket(apr_thread_t *thd, apr_pool_t * p, apr_socket_t * sock,
1006                          event_conn_state_t * cs, int my_child_num,
1007                          int my_thread_num)
1008{
1009    conn_rec *c;
1010    long conn_id = ID_FROM_CHILD_THREAD(my_child_num, my_thread_num);
1011    int clogging = 0;
1012    apr_status_t rv;
1013    int rc = OK;
1014
1015    if (cs == NULL) {           /* This is a new connection */ view japanese
1016        listener_poll_type *pt = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*pt));
1017        cs = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(event_conn_state_t));
1018        cs->bucket_alloc = apr_bucket_alloc_create(p);
1019        ap_create_sb_handle(&cs->sbh, p, my_child_num, my_thread_num);
1020        c = ap_run_create_connection(p, ap_server_conf, sock,
1021                                     conn_id, cs->sbh, cs->bucket_alloc);
1022        if (!c) {
1023            ap_queue_info_push_pool(worker_queue_info, p);
1024            return;
1025        }
1026        apr_atomic_inc32(&connection_count);
1027        apr_pool_cleanup_register(c->pool, cs, decrement_connection_count,
1028                                  apr_pool_cleanup_null);
1029        ap_set_module_config(c->conn_config, &mpm_event_module, cs);
1030        c->current_thread = thd;
1031        c->cs = &cs->pub;
1032        cs->c = c;
1033        cs->p = p;
1034        cs->sc = ap_get_module_config(ap_server_conf->module_config,
1035                                      &mpm_event_module);
1036        cs->pfd.desc_type = APR_POLL_SOCKET;
1037        cs->pfd.desc.s = sock;
1038        pt->type = PT_CSD;
1039        pt->baton = cs;
1040        cs->pfd.client_data = pt;
1041        apr_pool_pre_cleanup_register(p, cs, ptrans_pre_cleanup);
1042        TO_QUEUE_ELEM_INIT(cs);
1043
1044        ap_update_vhost_given_ip(c);
1045
1046        rc = ap_pre_connection(c, sock);
1047        if (rc != OK && rc != DONE) {
1048            ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, c, APLOGNO(00469)
1049                          "process_socket: connection aborted");
1050            close_connection(cs);
1051            return;
1052        }
1053
1054        /**
1055         * XXX If the platform does not have a usable way of bundling
1056         * accept() with a socket readability check, like Win32,
1057         * and there are measurable delays before the
1058         * socket is readable due to the first data packet arriving,
1059         * it might be better to create the cs on the listener thread
1060         * with the state set to CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE
1061         *
1062         * FreeBSD users will want to enable the HTTP accept filter
1063         * module in their kernel for the highest performance
1064         * When the accept filter is active, sockets are kept in the
1065         * kernel until a HTTP request is received.
1066         */ view japanese
1067        cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_PROCESSING;
1068        cs->pub.sense = CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT;
1069    }
1070    else {
1071        c = cs->c;
1072        ap_update_sb_handle(cs->sbh, my_child_num, my_thread_num);
1073        notify_resume(cs, 0);
1074        c->current_thread = thd;
1075        /* Subsequent request on a conn, and thread number is part of ID */ view japanese
1076        c->id = conn_id;
1077    }
1078
1079    if (CONN_STATE_IS_LINGERING_CLOSE(cs->pub.state)) {
1080        goto lingering_close;
1081    }
1082
1083    if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_PROCESSING
1084        /* If we have an input filter which 'clogs' the input stream,
1085         * like mod_ssl used to, lets just do the normal read from input
1086         * filters, like the Worker MPM does. Filters that need to write
1087         * where they would otherwise read, or read where they would
1088         * otherwise write, should set the sense appropriately.
1089         */ view japanese
1090         || c->clogging_input_filters) {
1091 process_connection:
1092        cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_PROCESSING;
1093
1094        clogging = c->clogging_input_filters;
1095        if (clogging) {
1096            apr_atomic_inc32(&clogged_count);
1097        }
1098        rc = ap_run_process_connection(c);
1099        if (clogging) {
1100            apr_atomic_dec32(&clogged_count);
1101        }
1102        /*
1103         * The process_connection hooks should set the appropriate connection
1104         * state upon return, for event MPM to either:
1105         * - CONN_STATE_LINGER: do lingering close;
1106         * - CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION: flush pending outputs using Timeout
1107         *   and wait for next incoming data using KeepAliveTimeout, then come
1108         *   back to process_connection() hooks;
1109         * - CONN_STATE_SUSPENDED: suspend the connection such that it now
1110         *   interacts with the MPM through suspend/resume_connection() hooks,
1111         *   and/or registered poll callbacks (PT_USER), and/or registered
1112         *   timed callbacks triggered by timer events;
1113         * - CONN_STATE_ASYNC_WAITIO: wait for read/write-ability of the underlying
1114         *   socket using Timeout and come back to process_connection() hooks when
1115         *   ready;
1116         * - CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE: now handled by CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION
1117         *   to flush before waiting for next data (that might depend on it).
1118         * If a process_connection hook returns an error or no hook sets the state
1119         * to one of the above expected value, forcibly close the connection w/
1120         * CONN_STATE_LINGER.  This covers the cases where no process_connection
1121         * hook executes (DECLINED), or one returns OK w/o touching the state (i.e.
1122         * CONN_STATE_PROCESSING remains after the call) which can happen with
1123         * third-party modules not updated to work specifically with event MPM
1124         * while this was expected to do lingering close unconditionally with
1125         * worker or prefork MPMs for instance.
1126         */ view japanese
1127        switch (rc) {
1128        case DONE:
1129            rc = OK; /* same as OK, fall through */ view japanese
1130        case OK:
1131            if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_PROCESSING) {
1132                cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1133            }
1134            else if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE) {
1135                cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION;
1136            }
1137            break;
1138        }
1139        if (rc != OK || (cs->pub.state != CONN_STATE_LINGER
1140                         && cs->pub.state != CONN_STATE_ASYNC_WAITIO
1141                         && cs->pub.state != CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION
1142                         && cs->pub.state != CONN_STATE_SUSPENDED)) {
1143            ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, c, APLOGNO(10111)
1144                          "process_socket: connection processing returned %i "
1145                          "(%sstate %i): closing",
1146                          rc, rc ? "" : "unexpected ", (int)cs->pub.state);
1147            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1148        }
1149        else if (c->aborted) {
1150            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1151        }
1152        if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_LINGER) {
1153            goto lingering_close;
1154        }
1155    }
1156
1157    if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_ASYNC_WAITIO) {
1158        /* Set a read/write timeout for this connection, and let the
1159         * event thread poll for read/writeability.
1160         */ view japanese
1161        cs->queue_timestamp = apr_time_now();
1162        notify_suspend(cs);
1163
1164        ap_update_child_status(cs->sbh, SERVER_BUSY_READ, NULL);
1165
1166        /* Modules might set c->cs->sense to CONN_SENSE_WANT_WRITE,
1167         * the default is CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ still.
1168         */ view japanese
1169        update_reqevents_from_sense(cs, CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ);
1170        apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1171        TO_QUEUE_APPEND(cs->sc->io_q, cs);
1172        rv = apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1173        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_EEXIST(rv)) {
1174            AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1175            TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(cs->sc->io_q, cs);
1176            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1177            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10503)
1178                         "process_socket: apr_pollset_add failure in "
1179                         "CONN_STATE_ASYNC_WAITIO");
1180            close_connection(cs);
1181            signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1182        }
1183        else {
1184            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1185        }
1186        return;
1187    }
1188
1189    if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION) {
1190        ap_filter_t *output_filter = c->output_filters;
1191        apr_status_t rv;
1192
1193        /* Flush all pending outputs before going to CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE or
1194         * straight to CONN_STATE_PROCESSING if inputs are pending already.
1195         */ view japanese
1196        
1197        ap_update_child_status(cs->sbh, SERVER_BUSY_WRITE, NULL);
1198        while (output_filter->next != NULL) {
1199            output_filter = output_filter->next;
1200        }
1201        rv = output_filter->frec->filter_func.out_func(output_filter, NULL);
1202        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1203            ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, c, APLOGNO(00470)
1204                          "network write failure in core output filter");
1205            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1206            goto lingering_close;
1207        }
1208        if (c->data_in_output_filters || cs->pub.sense == CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ) {
1209            /* Still in WRITE_COMPLETION_STATE:
1210             * Set a read/write timeout for this connection, and let the
1211             * event thread poll for read/writeability.
1212             */ view japanese
1213            cs->queue_timestamp = apr_time_now();
1214            notify_suspend(cs);
1215
1216            /* Add work to pollset. */
1217            update_reqevents_from_sense(cs, CONN_SENSE_WANT_WRITE);
1218            apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1219            TO_QUEUE_APPEND(cs->sc->wc_q, cs);
1220            rv = apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1221            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_EEXIST(rv)) {
1222                AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1223                TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(cs->sc->wc_q, cs);
1224                apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1225                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03465)
1226                             "process_socket: apr_pollset_add failure in "
1227                             "CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION");
1228                close_connection(cs);
1229                signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1230            }
1231            else {
1232                apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1233            }
1234            return;
1235        }
1236        if (c->keepalive != AP_CONN_KEEPALIVE || c->aborted) {
1237            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1238            goto lingering_close;
1239        }
1240        if (c->data_in_input_filters) {
1241            goto process_connection;
1242        }
1243        if (listener_may_exit) {
1244            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1245            goto lingering_close;
1246        }
1247
1248        /* Fall through */ view japanese
1249        cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE;
1250    }
1251
1252    if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE) {
1253        ap_update_child_status(cs->sbh, SERVER_BUSY_KEEPALIVE, NULL);
1254
1255        /* It greatly simplifies the logic to use a single timeout value per q
1256         * because the new element can just be added to the end of the list and
1257         * it will stay sorted in expiration time sequence.  If brand new
1258         * sockets are sent to the event thread for a readability check, this
1259         * will be a slight behavior change - they use the non-keepalive
1260         * timeout today.  With a normal client, the socket will be readable in
1261         * a few milliseconds anyway.
1262         */ view japanese
1263        cs->queue_timestamp = apr_time_now();
1264        notify_suspend(cs);
1265
1266        /* Add work to pollset. */ view japanese
1267        cs->pub.sense = CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT;
1268        update_reqevents_from_sense(cs, CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ);
1269        apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1270        TO_QUEUE_APPEND(cs->sc->ka_q, cs);
1271        rv = apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1272        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_EEXIST(rv)) {
1273            AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1274            TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(cs->sc->ka_q, cs);
1275            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1276            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03093)
1277                         "process_socket: apr_pollset_add failure for "
1278                         "keep alive");
1279            close_connection(cs);
1280            signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1281        }
1282        else {
1283            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1284        }
1285        return;
1286    }
1287
1288    if (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_SUSPENDED) {
1289        apr_atomic_inc32(&suspended_count);
1290        notify_suspend(cs);
1291        return;
1292    }
1293
1294 lingering_close:
1295    /* CONN_STATE_LINGER[_*] fall through process_lingering_close() */ view japanese
1296    process_lingering_close(cs);
1297}
1298
1299/* conns_this_child has gone to zero or below.  See if the admin coded
1300   "MaxConnectionsPerChild 0", and keep going in that case.  Doing it this way
1301   simplifies the hot path in worker_thread */ view japanese
1302static void check_infinite_requests(void)
1303{
1304    if (ap_max_requests_per_child) {
1305        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
1306                     "Stopping process due to MaxConnectionsPerChild");
1307        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1308    }
1309    /* keep going */ view japanese
1310    conns_this_child = APR_INT32_MAX;
1311}
1312
1313static int close_listeners(int *closed)
1314{
1315    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, ap_server_conf,
1316                 "clos%s listeners (connection_count=%u)",
1317                 *closed ? "ed" : "ing", apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count));
1318    if (!*closed) {
1319        int i;
1320
1321        ap_close_listeners_ex(my_bucket->listeners);
1322        *closed = 1; /* once */ view japanese
1323
1324        dying = 1;
1325        ap_scoreboard_image->parent[ap_child_slot].quiescing = 1;
1326        for (i = 0; i < threads_per_child; ++i) {
1327            ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(ap_child_slot, i,
1328                                                SERVER_GRACEFUL, NULL);
1329        }
1330        /* wake up the main thread */ view japanese
1331        kill(ap_my_pid, SIGTERM);
1332
1333        ap_queue_info_free_idle_pools(worker_queue_info);
1334        ap_queue_interrupt_all(worker_queue);
1335
1336        return 1;
1337    }
1338    return 0;
1339}
1340
1341static void unblock_signal(int sig)
1342{
1343    sigset_t sig_mask;
1344
1345    sigemptyset(&sig_mask);
1346    sigaddset(&sig_mask, sig);
1347#if defined(SIGPROCMASK_SETS_THREAD_MASK)
1348    sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &sig_mask, NULL);
1349#else
1350    pthread_sigmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &sig_mask, NULL);
1351#endif
1352}
1353
1354static void dummy_signal_handler(int sig)
1355{
1356    /* XXX If specifying SIG_IGN is guaranteed to unblock a syscall,
1357     *     then we don't need this goofy function.
1358     */ view japanese
1359}
1360
1361
1362static apr_status_t push_timer2worker(timer_event_t* te)
1363{
1364    return ap_queue_push_timer(worker_queue, te);
1365}
1366
1367/*
1368 * Pre-condition: cs is neither in event_pollset nor a timeout queue
1369 * this function may only be called by the listener
1370 */ view japanese
1371static apr_status_t push2worker(event_conn_state_t *cs, apr_socket_t *csd,
1372                                apr_pool_t *ptrans)
1373{
1374    apr_status_t rc;
1375
1376    if (cs) {
1377        csd = cs->pfd.desc.s;
1378        ptrans = cs->p;
1379    }
1380    rc = ap_queue_push_socket(worker_queue, csd, cs, ptrans);
1381    if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
1382        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rc, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00471)
1383                     "push2worker: ap_queue_push_socket failed");
1384        /* trash the connection; we couldn't queue the connected
1385         * socket to a worker
1386         */ view japanese
1387        if (cs) {
1388            shutdown_connection(cs);
1389        }
1390        else {
1391            if (csd) {
1392                close_socket_nonblocking(csd);
1393            }
1394            if (ptrans) {
1395                ap_queue_info_push_pool(worker_queue_info, ptrans);
1396            }
1397        }
1398        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1399    }
1400
1401    return rc;
1402}
1403
1404/* get_worker:
1405 *     If *have_idle_worker_p == 0, reserve a worker thread, and set
1406 *     *have_idle_worker_p = 1.
1407 *     If *have_idle_worker_p is already 1, will do nothing.
1408 *     If blocking == 1, block if all workers are currently busy.
1409 *     If no worker was available immediately, will set *all_busy to 1.
1410 *     XXX: If there are no workers, we should not block immediately but
1411 *     XXX: close all keep-alive connections first.
1412 */ view japanese
1413static void get_worker(int *have_idle_worker_p, int blocking, int *all_busy)
1414{
1415    apr_status_t rc;
1416
1417    if (*have_idle_worker_p) {
1418        /* already reserved a worker thread - must have hit a
1419         * transient error on a previous pass
1420         */ view japanese
1421        return;
1422    }
1423
1424    if (blocking)
1425        rc = ap_queue_info_wait_for_idler(worker_queue_info, all_busy);
1426    else
1427        rc = ap_queue_info_try_get_idler(worker_queue_info);
1428
1429    if (rc == APR_SUCCESS || APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(rc)) {
1430        *have_idle_worker_p = 1;
1431    }
1432    else if (!blocking && rc == APR_EAGAIN) {
1433        *all_busy = 1;
1434    }
1435    else {
1436        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00472)
1437                     "ap_queue_info_wait_for_idler failed.  "
1438                     "Attempting to shutdown process gracefully");
1439        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1440    }
1441}
1442
1443/* Structures to reuse */ view japanese
1444static timer_event_t timer_free_ring;
1445
1446static apr_skiplist *timer_skiplist;
1447static volatile apr_time_t timers_next_expiry;
1448
1449/* Same goal as for TIMEOUT_FUDGE_FACTOR (avoid extra poll calls), but applied
1450 * to timers. Since their timeouts are custom (user defined), we can't be too
1451 * approximative here (hence using 0.01s).
1452 */ view japanese
1453#define EVENT_FUDGE_FACTOR apr_time_from_msec(10)
1454
1455/* The following compare function is used by apr_skiplist_insert() to keep the
1456 * elements (timers) sorted and provide O(log n) complexity (this is also true
1457 * for apr_skiplist_{find,remove}(), but those are not used in MPM event where
1458 * inserted timers are not searched nor removed, but with apr_skiplist_pop()
1459 * which does use any compare function).  It is meant to return 0 when a == b,
1460 * <0 when a < b, and >0 when a > b.  However apr_skiplist_insert() will not
1461 * add duplicates (i.e. a == b), and apr_skiplist_add() is only available in
1462 * APR 1.6, yet multiple timers could possibly be created in the same micro-
1463 * second (duplicates with regard to apr_time_t); therefore we implement the
1464 * compare function to return +1 instead of 0 when compared timers are equal,
1465 * thus duplicates are still added after each other (in order of insertion).
1466 */ view japanese
1467static int timer_comp(void *a, void *b)
1468{
1469    apr_time_t t1 = (apr_time_t) ((timer_event_t *)a)->when;
1470    apr_time_t t2 = (apr_time_t) ((timer_event_t *)b)->when;
1471    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(t1);
1472    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(t2);
1473    return ((t1 < t2) ? -1 : 1);
1474}
1475
1476static apr_thread_mutex_t *g_timer_skiplist_mtx;
1477
1478static apr_status_t event_register_timed_callback(apr_time_t t,
1479                                                  ap_mpm_callback_fn_t *cbfn,
1480                                                  void *baton)
1481{
1482    timer_event_t *te;
1483    /* oh yeah, and make locking smarter/fine grained. */ view japanese
1484    apr_thread_mutex_lock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
1485
1486    if (!APR_RING_EMPTY(&timer_free_ring.link, timer_event_t, link)) {
1487        te = APR_RING_FIRST(&timer_free_ring.link);
1488        APR_RING_REMOVE(te, link);
1489    }
1490    else {
1491        te = apr_skiplist_alloc(timer_skiplist, sizeof(timer_event_t));
1492        APR_RING_ELEM_INIT(te, link);
1493    }
1494
1495    te->cbfunc = cbfn;
1496    te->baton = baton;
1497    /* XXXXX: optimize */ view japanese
1498    te->when = t + apr_time_now();
1499
1500    { 
1501        apr_time_t next_expiry;
1502
1503        /* Okay, add sorted by when.. */ view japanese
1504        apr_skiplist_insert(timer_skiplist, te);
1505
1506        /* Cheaply update the global timers_next_expiry with this event's
1507         * if it expires before.
1508         */ view japanese
1509        next_expiry = timers_next_expiry;
1510        if (!next_expiry || next_expiry > te->when + EVENT_FUDGE_FACTOR) {
1511            timers_next_expiry = te->when;
1512            /* Unblock the poll()ing listener for it to update its timeout. */ view japanese
1513            if (listener_is_wakeable) {
1514                apr_pollset_wakeup(event_pollset);
1515            }
1516        }
1517    }
1518
1519    apr_thread_mutex_unlock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
1520
1521    return APR_SUCCESS;
1522}
1523
1524
1525/*
1526 * Flush data and close our side of the connection, then drain incoming data.
1527 * If the latter would block put the connection in one of the linger timeout
1528 * queues to be called back when ready, and repeat until it's closed by peer.
1529 * Only to be called in the worker thread, and since it's in immediate call
1530 * stack, we can afford a comfortable buffer size to consume data quickly.
1531 * Pre-condition: cs is not in any timeout queue and not in the pollset,
1532 *                timeout_mutex is not locked
1533 */ view japanese
1534#define LINGERING_BUF_SIZE (32 * 1024)
1535static void process_lingering_close(event_conn_state_t *cs)
1536{
1537    apr_socket_t *csd = ap_get_conn_socket(cs->c);
1538    char dummybuf[LINGERING_BUF_SIZE];
1539    apr_size_t nbytes;
1540    apr_status_t rv;
1541    struct timeout_queue *q;
1542
1543    ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, cs->c,
1544                  "lingering close from state %i", (int)cs->pub.state);
1545    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(CONN_STATE_IS_LINGERING_CLOSE(cs->pub.state));
1546
1547    if (!cs->linger_started) {
1548        cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER;
1549        cs->linger_started = 1;
1550
1551        /* defer_lingering_close() may have bumped lingering_count already */ view japanese
1552        if (!cs->deferred_linger) {
1553            apr_atomic_inc32(&lingering_count);
1554        }
1555
1556        apr_socket_timeout_set(csd, apr_time_from_sec(SECONDS_TO_LINGER));
1557        if (ap_start_lingering_close(cs->c)) {
1558            notify_suspend(cs);
1559            close_connection(cs);
1560            return;
1561        }
1562        
1563        /* All nonblocking from now, no need for APR_INCOMPLETE_READ either */ view japanese
1564        apr_socket_timeout_set(csd, 0);
1565        apr_socket_opt_set(csd, APR_INCOMPLETE_READ, 0);
1566
1567        /*
1568         * If some module requested a shortened waiting period, only wait for
1569         * 2s (SECONDS_TO_LINGER). This is useful for mitigating certain
1570         * DoS attacks.
1571         */ view japanese
1572        if (apr_table_get(cs->c->notes, "short-lingering-close")) {
1573            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER_SHORT;
1574        }
1575        else {
1576            cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_LINGER_NORMAL;
1577        }
1578        cs->pub.sense = CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT;
1579        notify_suspend(cs);
1580
1581        /* One timestamp/duration for the whole lingering close time.
1582         * XXX: This makes the (short_)linger_q not sorted/ordered by expiring
1583         * timeouts whenever multiple schedules are necessary (EAGAIN below),
1584         * but we probabaly don't care since these connections do not count
1585         * for connections_above_limit() and all of them will be killed when
1586         * busy or gracefully stopping anyway.
1587         */ view japanese
1588        cs->queue_timestamp = apr_time_now();
1589    }
1590
1591    do {
1592        nbytes = sizeof(dummybuf);
1593        rv = apr_socket_recv(csd, dummybuf, &nbytes);
1594    } while (rv == APR_SUCCESS);
1595
1596    if (!APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(rv)) {
1597        close_connection(cs);
1598        return;
1599    }
1600
1601    /* (Re)queue the connection to come back when readable */ view japanese
1602    update_reqevents_from_sense(cs, CONN_SENSE_WANT_READ);
1603    q = (cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_LINGER_SHORT) ? short_linger_q : linger_q;
1604    apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1605    TO_QUEUE_APPEND(q, cs);
1606    rv = apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1607    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_EEXIST(rv)) {
1608        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1609        TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(q, cs);
1610        apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1611        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03092)
1612                     "process_lingering_close: apr_pollset_add failure");
1613        close_connection(cs);
1614        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1615        return;
1616    }
1617    apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1618}
1619
1620/* call 'func' for all elements of 'q' above 'expiry'.
1621 * Pre-condition: timeout_mutex must already be locked
1622 * Post-condition: timeout_mutex will be locked again
1623 */ view japanese
1624static void process_timeout_queue(struct timeout_queue *q, apr_time_t expiry,
1625                                  int (*func)(event_conn_state_t *))
1626{
1627    apr_uint32_t total = 0, count;
1628    event_conn_state_t *first, *cs, *last;
1629    struct event_conn_state_t trash;
1630    struct timeout_queue *qp;
1631    apr_status_t rv;
1632
1633    if (!*q->total) {
1634        return;
1635    }
1636
1637    APR_RING_INIT(&trash.timeout_list, event_conn_state_t, timeout_list);
1638    for (qp = q; qp; qp = qp->next) {
1639        count = 0;
1640        cs = first = last = APR_RING_FIRST(&qp->head);
1641        while (cs != APR_RING_SENTINEL(&qp->head, event_conn_state_t,
1642                                       timeout_list)) {
1643            /* Trash the entry if:
1644             * - no expiry was given (zero means all), or
1645             * - it expired (according to the queue timeout), or
1646             * - the system clock skewed in the past: no entry should be
1647             *   registered above the given expiry (~now) + the queue
1648             *   timeout, we won't keep any here (eg. for centuries).
1649             *
1650             * Otherwise stop, no following entry will match thanks to the
1651             * single timeout per queue (entries are added to the end!).
1652             * This allows maintenance in O(1).
1653             */ view japanese
1654            if (expiry && cs->queue_timestamp + qp->timeout > expiry
1655                       && cs->queue_timestamp < expiry + qp->timeout) {
1656                /* Since this is the next expiring entry of this queue, update
1657                 * the global queues_next_expiry if it's later than this one.
1658                 */ view japanese
1659                apr_time_t elem_expiry = cs->queue_timestamp + qp->timeout;
1660                apr_time_t next_expiry = queues_next_expiry;
1661                if (!next_expiry
1662                        || next_expiry > elem_expiry + TIMEOUT_FUDGE_FACTOR) {
1663                    queues_next_expiry = elem_expiry;
1664                }
1665                break;
1666            }
1667
1668            last = cs;
1669            rv = apr_pollset_remove(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1670            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_NOTFOUND(rv)) {
1671                AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1672                ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, cs->c, APLOGNO(00473)
1673                              "apr_pollset_remove failed");
1674            }
1675            cs = APR_RING_NEXT(cs, timeout_list);
1676            count++;
1677        }
1678        if (!count)
1679            continue;
1680
1681        APR_RING_UNSPLICE(first, last, timeout_list);
1682        APR_RING_SPLICE_TAIL(&trash.timeout_list, first, last, event_conn_state_t,
1683                             timeout_list);
1684        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(*q->total >= count && qp->count >= count);
1685        *q->total -= count;
1686        qp->count -= count;
1687        total += count;
1688    }
1689    if (!total)
1690        return;
1691
1692    apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1693    first = APR_RING_FIRST(&trash.timeout_list);
1694    do {
1695        cs = APR_RING_NEXT(first, timeout_list);
1696        TO_QUEUE_ELEM_INIT(first);
1697        func(first);
1698        first = cs;
1699    } while (--total);
1700    apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1701}
1702
1703static void process_keepalive_queue(apr_time_t expiry)
1704{
1705    /* If all workers are busy, we kill older keep-alive connections so
1706     * that they may connect to another process.
1707     */ view japanese
1708    if (!expiry && *keepalive_q->total) {
1709        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
1710                     "All workers are busy or dying, will shutdown %u "
1711                     "keep-alive connections", *keepalive_q->total);
1712    }
1713    process_timeout_queue(keepalive_q, expiry, shutdown_connection);
1714}
1715
1716static void * APR_THREAD_FUNC listener_thread(apr_thread_t * thd, void *dummy)
1717{
1718    apr_status_t rc;
1719    proc_info *ti = dummy;
1720    int process_slot = ti->pslot;
1721    struct process_score *ps = ap_get_scoreboard_process(process_slot);
1722    int closed = 0;
1723    int have_idle_worker = 0;
1724    apr_time_t last_log;
1725
1726    last_log = apr_time_now();
1727    free(ti);
1728
1729    /* Unblock the signal used to wake this thread up, and set a handler for
1730     * it.
1731     */ view japanese
1732    apr_signal(LISTENER_SIGNAL, dummy_signal_handler);
1733    unblock_signal(LISTENER_SIGNAL);
1734
1735    for (;;) {
1736        timer_event_t *te;
1737        const apr_pollfd_t *out_pfd;
1738        apr_int32_t num = 0;
1739        apr_interval_time_t timeout;
1740        apr_time_t now, expiry = -1;
1741        int workers_were_busy = 0;
1742
1743        if (conns_this_child <= 0)
1744            check_infinite_requests();
1745
1746        if (listener_may_exit) {
1747            int first_close = close_listeners(&closed);
1748
1749            if (terminate_mode == ST_UNGRACEFUL
1750                || apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count) == 0)
1751                break;
1752
1753            /* Don't wait in poll() for the first close (i.e. dying now), we
1754             * want to maintain the queues and schedule defer_linger_chain ASAP
1755             * to kill kept-alive connection and shutdown the workers and child
1756             * faster.
1757             */ view japanese
1758            if (first_close) {
1759                goto do_maintenance; /* with expiry == -1 */ view japanese
1760            }
1761        }
1762
1763        now = apr_time_now();
1764        if (APLOGtrace6(ap_server_conf)) {
1765            /* trace log status every second */ view japanese
1766            if (now - last_log > apr_time_from_sec(1)) {
1767                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, ap_server_conf,
1768                             "connections: %u (waitio:%u write-completion:%u"
1769                             "keep-alive:%u lingering:%u suspended:%u clogged:%u), "
1770                             "workers: %u/%u shutdown",
1771                             apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count),
1772                             apr_atomic_read32(waitio_q->total),
1773                             apr_atomic_read32(write_completion_q->total),
1774                             apr_atomic_read32(keepalive_q->total),
1775                             apr_atomic_read32(&lingering_count),
1776                             apr_atomic_read32(&suspended_count),
1777                             apr_atomic_read32(&clogged_count),
1778                             apr_atomic_read32(&threads_shutdown),
1779                             threads_per_child);
1780                last_log = now;
1781            }
1782        }
1783
1784        /* Start with an infinite poll() timeout and update it according to
1785         * the next expiring timer or queue entry. If there are none, either
1786         * the listener is wakeable and it can poll() indefinitely until a wake
1787         * up occurs, otherwise periodic checks (maintenance, shutdown, ...)
1788         * must be performed.
1789         */ view japanese
1790        now = apr_time_now();
1791        timeout = -1;
1792
1793        /* Push expired timers to a worker, the first remaining one determines
1794         * the maximum time to poll() below, if any.
1795         */ view japanese
1796        expiry = timers_next_expiry;
1797        if (expiry && expiry < now) {
1798            apr_thread_mutex_lock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
1799            while ((te = apr_skiplist_peek(timer_skiplist))) {
1800                if (te->when > now) {
1801                    timers_next_expiry = te->when;
1802                    timeout = te->when - now;
1803                    break;
1804                }
1805                apr_skiplist_pop(timer_skiplist, NULL);
1806                push_timer2worker(te);
1807            }
1808            if (!te) {
1809                timers_next_expiry = 0;
1810            }
1811            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
1812        }
1813
1814        /* Same for queues, use their next expiry, if any. */ view japanese
1815        expiry = queues_next_expiry;
1816        if (expiry
1817                && (timeout < 0
1818                    || expiry <= now
1819                    || timeout > expiry - now)) {
1820            timeout = expiry > now ? expiry - now : 0;
1821        }
1822
1823        /* When non-wakeable, don't wait more than 100 ms, in any case. */ view japanese
1824#define NON_WAKEABLE_POLL_TIMEOUT apr_time_from_msec(100)
1825        if (!listener_is_wakeable
1826                && (timeout < 0
1827                    || timeout > NON_WAKEABLE_POLL_TIMEOUT)) {
1828            timeout = NON_WAKEABLE_POLL_TIMEOUT;
1829        }
1830        else if (timeout > 0) {
1831            /* apr_pollset_poll() might round down the timeout to milliseconds,
1832             * let's forcibly round up here to never return before the timeout.
1833             */ view japanese
1834            timeout = apr_time_from_msec(
1835                apr_time_as_msec(timeout + apr_time_from_msec(1) - 1)
1836            );
1837        }
1838
1839        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE7, 0, ap_server_conf,
1840                     "polling with timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT
1841                     " queues_timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT
1842                     " timers_timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT,
1843                     timeout, queues_next_expiry - now,
1844                     timers_next_expiry - now);
1845
1846        rc = apr_pollset_poll(event_pollset, timeout, &num, &out_pfd);
1847        if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
1848            if (!APR_STATUS_IS_EINTR(rc) && !APR_STATUS_IS_TIMEUP(rc)) {
1849                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rc, ap_server_conf,
1850                             "apr_pollset_poll failed.  Attempting to "
1851                             "shutdown process gracefully");
1852                signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1853            }
1854            num = 0;
1855        }
1856
1857        if (APLOGtrace7(ap_server_conf)) {
1858            now = apr_time_now();
1859            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE7, rc, ap_server_conf,
1860                         "polled with num=%u exit=%d/%d conns=%d"
1861                         " queues_timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT
1862                         " timers_timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT,
1863                         num, listener_may_exit, dying,
1864                         apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count),
1865                         queues_next_expiry - now, timers_next_expiry - now);
1866        }
1867
1868        /* XXX possible optimization: stash the current time for use as
1869         * r->request_time for new requests or queues maintenance
1870         */ view japanese
1871
1872        for (; num; --num, ++out_pfd) {
1873            listener_poll_type *pt = (listener_poll_type *) out_pfd->client_data;
1874            if (pt->type == PT_CSD) {
1875                /* one of the sockets is readable */ view japanese
1876                event_conn_state_t *cs = (event_conn_state_t *) pt->baton;
1877                struct timeout_queue *remove_from_q = NULL;
1878                /* don't wait for a worker for a keepalive request or
1879                 * lingering close processing. */ view japanese
1880                int blocking = 0;
1881
1882                switch (cs->pub.state) {
1883                case CONN_STATE_WRITE_COMPLETION:
1884                    remove_from_q = cs->sc->wc_q;
1885                    blocking = 1;
1886                    break;
1887
1888                case CONN_STATE_ASYNC_WAITIO:
1889                    cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_PROCESSING;
1890                    remove_from_q = cs->sc->io_q;
1891                    blocking = 1;
1892                    break;
1893
1894                case CONN_STATE_KEEPALIVE:
1895                    cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_PROCESSING;
1896                    remove_from_q = cs->sc->ka_q;
1897                    break;
1898
1899                case CONN_STATE_LINGER_NORMAL:
1900                    remove_from_q = linger_q;
1901                    break;
1902
1903                case CONN_STATE_LINGER_SHORT:
1904                    remove_from_q = short_linger_q;
1905                    break;
1906
1907                default:
1908                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rc,
1909                                 ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03096)
1910                                 "event_loop: unexpected state %d",
1911                                 cs->pub.state);
1912                    ap_assert(0);
1913                }
1914
1915                if (remove_from_q) {
1916                    apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
1917                    TO_QUEUE_REMOVE(remove_from_q, cs);
1918                    rc = apr_pollset_remove(event_pollset, &cs->pfd);
1919                    apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
1920                    /*
1921                     * Some of the pollset backends, like KQueue or Epoll
1922                     * automagically remove the FD if the socket is closed,
1923                     * therefore, we can accept _SUCCESS or _NOTFOUND,
1924                     * and we still want to keep going
1925                     */ view japanese
1926                    if (rc != APR_SUCCESS && !APR_STATUS_IS_NOTFOUND(rc)) {
1927                        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
1928                        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, ap_server_conf,
1929                                     APLOGNO(03094) "pollset remove failed");
1930                        close_connection(cs);
1931                        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1932                        break;
1933                    }
1934
1935                    /* If we don't get a worker immediately (nonblocking), we
1936                     * close the connection; the client can re-connect to a
1937                     * different process for keepalive, and for lingering close
1938                     * the connection will be shutdown so the choice is to favor
1939                     * incoming/alive connections.
1940                     */ view japanese
1941                    get_worker(&have_idle_worker, blocking,
1942                               &workers_were_busy);
1943                    if (!have_idle_worker) {
1944                        shutdown_connection(cs);
1945                    }
1946                    else if (push2worker(cs, NULL, NULL) == APR_SUCCESS) {
1947                        have_idle_worker = 0;
1948                    }
1949                }
1950            }
1951            else if (pt->type == PT_ACCEPT && !listeners_disabled()) {
1952                /* A Listener Socket is ready for an accept() */ view japanese
1953                if (workers_were_busy) {
1954                    disable_listensocks();
1955                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
1956                                 "All workers busy, not accepting new conns "
1957                                 "in this process");
1958                }
1959                else if (connections_above_limit(&workers_were_busy)) {
1960                    disable_listensocks();
1961                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
1962                                 "Too many open connections (%u), "
1963                                 "not accepting new conns in this process",
1964                                 apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count));
1965                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
1966                                 "Idle workers: %u",
1967                                 ap_queue_info_num_idlers(worker_queue_info));
1968                }
1969                else if (!listener_may_exit) {
1970                    void *csd = NULL;
1971                    ap_listen_rec *lr = (ap_listen_rec *) pt->baton;
1972                    apr_pool_t *ptrans;         /* Pool for per-transaction stuff */
1973                    ap_queue_info_pop_pool(worker_queue_info, &ptrans);
1974
1975                    if (ptrans == NULL) {
1976                        /* create a new transaction pool for each accepted socket */ view japanese
1977                        apr_allocator_t *allocator = NULL;
1978
1979                        rc = apr_allocator_create(&allocator);
1980                        if (rc == APR_SUCCESS) {
1981                            apr_allocator_max_free_set(allocator,
1982                                                       ap_max_mem_free);
1983                            rc = apr_pool_create_ex(&ptrans, pconf, NULL,
1984                                                    allocator);
1985                            if (rc == APR_SUCCESS) {
1986                                apr_pool_tag(ptrans, "transaction");
1987                                apr_allocator_owner_set(allocator, ptrans);
1988                            }
1989                        }
1990                        if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
1991                            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rc,
1992                                         ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03097)
1993                                         "Failed to create transaction pool");
1994                            if (allocator) {
1995                                apr_allocator_destroy(allocator);
1996                            }
1997                            resource_shortage = 1;
1998                            signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
1999                            continue;
2000                        }
2001                    }
2002
2003                    get_worker(&have_idle_worker, 1, &workers_were_busy);
2004                    rc = lr->accept_func(&csd, lr, ptrans);
2005
2006                    /* later we trash rv and rely on csd to indicate
2007                     * success/failure
2008                     */ view japanese
2009                    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(rc == APR_SUCCESS || !csd);
2010
2011                    if (rc == APR_EGENERAL) {
