2012                        /* E[NM]FILE, ENOMEM, etc */ view japanese
2013                        resource_shortage = 1;
2014                        signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
2015                    }
2016
2017                    if (csd != NULL) {
2018                        conns_this_child--;
2019                        if (push2worker(NULL, csd, ptrans) == APR_SUCCESS) {
2020                            have_idle_worker = 0;
2021                        }
2022                    }
2023                    else {
2024                        ap_queue_info_push_pool(worker_queue_info, ptrans);
2025                    }
2026                }
2027            }               /* if:else on pt->type */ view japanese
2028        } /* for processing poll */ view japanese
2029
2030        /* We process the timeout queues here only when the global
2031         * queues_next_expiry is passed. This happens accurately since
2032         * adding to the queues (in workers) can only decrease this expiry,
2033         * while latest ones are only taken into account here (in listener)
2034         * during queues' processing, with the lock held. This works both
2035         * with and without wake-ability.
2036         */ view japanese
2037        expiry = queues_next_expiry;
2038do_maintenance:
2039        if (expiry && expiry < (now = apr_time_now())) {
2040            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE7, 0, ap_server_conf,
2041                         "queues maintenance with timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT,
2042                         expiry > 0 ? expiry - now : -1);
2043            apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
2044
2045            /* Steps below will recompute this. */ view japanese
2046            queues_next_expiry = 0;
2047
2048            /* Step 1: keepalive queue timeouts are closed */ view japanese
2049            if (workers_were_busy || dying) {
2050                process_keepalive_queue(0); /* kill'em all \m/ */
2051            }
2052            else {
2053                process_keepalive_queue(now);
2054            }
2055
2056            /* Step 2: waitio queue timeouts are flushed */ view japanese
2057            process_timeout_queue(waitio_q, now, defer_lingering_close);
2058
2059            /* Step 3: write completion queue timeouts are flushed */ view japanese
2060            process_timeout_queue(write_completion_q, now, defer_lingering_close);
2061
2062            /* Step 4: normal lingering close queue timeouts are closed */ view japanese
2063            if (dying && linger_q->timeout > short_linger_q->timeout) {
2064                /* Dying, force short timeout for normal lingering close */ view japanese
2065                linger_q->timeout = short_linger_q->timeout;
2066            }
2067            process_timeout_queue(linger_q, now, shutdown_connection);
2068
2069            /* Step 5: short lingering close queue timeouts are closed */ view japanese
2070            process_timeout_queue(short_linger_q, now, shutdown_connection);
2071
2072            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
2073            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE7, 0, ap_server_conf,
2074                         "queues maintained with timeout=%" APR_TIME_T_FMT,
2075                         queues_next_expiry > now ? queues_next_expiry - now
2076                                                  : -1);
2077
2078            ps->wait_io = apr_atomic_read32(waitio_q->total);
2079            ps->write_completion = apr_atomic_read32(write_completion_q->total);
2080            ps->keep_alive = apr_atomic_read32(keepalive_q->total);
2081            ps->lingering_close = apr_atomic_read32(&lingering_count);
2082            ps->suspended = apr_atomic_read32(&suspended_count);
2083            ps->connections = apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count);
2084        }
2085        else if ((workers_were_busy || dying)
2086                 && apr_atomic_read32(keepalive_q->total)) {
2087            apr_thread_mutex_lock(timeout_mutex);
2088            process_keepalive_queue(0); /* kill'em all \m/ */ view japanese
2089            apr_thread_mutex_unlock(timeout_mutex);
2090            ps->keep_alive = 0;
2091        }
2092
2093        /* If there are some lingering closes to defer (to a worker), schedule
2094         * them now. We might wakeup a worker spuriously if another one empties
2095         * defer_linger_chain in the meantime, but there also may be no active
2096         * or all busy workers for an undefined time.  In any case a deferred
2097         * lingering close can't starve if we do that here since the chain is
2098         * filled only above in the listener and it's emptied only in the
2099         * worker(s); thus a NULL here means it will stay so while the listener
2100         * waits (possibly indefinitely) in poll().
2101         */ view japanese
2102        if (defer_linger_chain) {
2103            get_worker(&have_idle_worker, 0, &workers_were_busy);
2104            if (have_idle_worker
2105                    && defer_linger_chain /* re-test */ view japanese
2106                    && push2worker(NULL, NULL, NULL) == APR_SUCCESS) {
2107                have_idle_worker = 0;
2108            }
2109        }
2110
2111        if (!workers_were_busy && should_enable_listensocks()) {
2112            enable_listensocks();
2113        }
2114    } /* listener main loop */ view japanese
2115
2116    ap_queue_term(worker_queue);
2117
2118    apr_thread_exit(thd, APR_SUCCESS);
2119    return NULL;
2120}
2121
2122/*
2123 * During graceful shutdown, if there are more running worker threads than
2124 * open connections, exit one worker thread.
2125 *
2126 * return 1 if thread should exit, 0 if it should continue running.
2127 */ view japanese
2128static int worker_thread_should_exit_early(void)
2129{
2130    for (;;) {
2131        apr_uint32_t conns = apr_atomic_read32(&connection_count);
2132        apr_uint32_t dead = apr_atomic_read32(&threads_shutdown);
2133        apr_uint32_t newdead;
2134
2135        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(dead <= threads_per_child);
2136        if (conns >= threads_per_child - dead)
2137            return 0;
2138
2139        newdead = dead + 1;
2140        if (apr_atomic_cas32(&threads_shutdown, newdead, dead) == dead) {
2141            /*
2142             * No other thread has exited in the mean time, safe to exit
2143             * this one.
2144             */ view japanese
2145            return 1;
2146        }
2147    }
2148}
2149
2150/* XXX For ungraceful termination/restart, we definitely don't want to
2151 *     wait for active connections to finish but we may want to wait
2152 *     for idle workers to get out of the queue code and release mutexes,
2153 *     since those mutexes are cleaned up pretty soon and some systems
2154 *     may not react favorably (i.e., segfault) if operations are attempted
2155 *     on cleaned-up mutexes.
2156 */ view japanese
2157static void *APR_THREAD_FUNC worker_thread(apr_thread_t * thd, void *dummy)
2158{
2159    proc_info *ti = dummy;
2160    int process_slot = ti->pslot;
2161    int thread_slot = ti->tslot;
2162    apr_status_t rv;
2163    int is_idle = 0;
2164
2165    free(ti);
2166
2167    ap_scoreboard_image->servers[process_slot][thread_slot].pid = ap_my_pid;
2168    ap_scoreboard_image->servers[process_slot][thread_slot].tid = apr_os_thread_current();
2169    ap_scoreboard_image->servers[process_slot][thread_slot].generation = retained->mpm->my_generation;
2170    ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(process_slot, thread_slot,
2171                                        SERVER_STARTING, NULL);
2172
2173    for (;;) {
2174        apr_socket_t *csd = NULL;
2175        event_conn_state_t *cs;
2176        timer_event_t *te = NULL;
2177        apr_pool_t *ptrans;         /* Pool for per-transaction stuff */ view japanese
2178
2179        if (!is_idle) {
2180            rv = ap_queue_info_set_idle(worker_queue_info, NULL);
2181            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2182                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_EMERG, rv, ap_server_conf,
2183                             "ap_queue_info_set_idle failed. Attempting to "
2184                             "shutdown process gracefully.");
2185                signal_threads(ST_GRACEFUL);
2186                break;
2187            }
2188            /* A new idler may have changed connections_above_limit(),
2189             * let the listener know and decide.
2190             */ view japanese
2191            if (listener_is_wakeable && should_enable_listensocks()) {
2192                apr_pollset_wakeup(event_pollset);
2193            }
2194            is_idle = 1;
2195        }
2196
2197        ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(process_slot, thread_slot,
2198                                            dying ? SERVER_GRACEFUL
2199                                                  : SERVER_READY, NULL);
2200      worker_pop:
2201        if (workers_may_exit) {
2202            break;
2203        }
2204        if (dying && worker_thread_should_exit_early()) {
2205            break;
2206        }
2207
2208        rv = ap_queue_pop_something(worker_queue, &csd, (void **)&cs,
2209                                    &ptrans, &te);
2210
2211        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2212            /* We get APR_EOF during a graceful shutdown once all the
2213             * connections accepted by this server process have been handled.
2214             */ view japanese
2215            if (APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(rv)) {
2216                break;
2217            }
2218            /* We get APR_EINTR whenever ap_queue_pop_*() has been interrupted
2219             * from an explicit call to ap_queue_interrupt_all(). This allows
2220             * us to unblock threads stuck in ap_queue_pop_*() when a shutdown
2221             * is pending.
2222             *
2223             * If workers_may_exit is set and this is ungraceful termination/
2224             * restart, we are bound to get an error on some systems (e.g.,
2225             * AIX, which sanity-checks mutex operations) since the queue
2226             * may have already been cleaned up.  Don't log the "error" if
2227             * workers_may_exit is set.
2228             */ view japanese
2229            else if (APR_STATUS_IS_EINTR(rv)) {
2230                goto worker_pop;
2231            }
2232            /* We got some other error. */ view japanese
2233            else if (!workers_may_exit) {
2234                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, ap_server_conf,
2235                             APLOGNO(03099) "ap_queue_pop_socket failed");
2236            }
2237            continue;
2238        }
2239        if (te != NULL) {
2240            te->cbfunc(te->baton);
2241
2242            {
2243                apr_thread_mutex_lock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
2244                APR_RING_INSERT_TAIL(&timer_free_ring.link, te, timer_event_t, link);
2245                apr_thread_mutex_unlock(g_timer_skiplist_mtx);
2246            }
2247        }
2248        else {
2249            is_idle = 0;
2250            if (csd != NULL) {
2251                worker_sockets[thread_slot] = csd;
2252                process_socket(thd, ptrans, csd, cs, process_slot, thread_slot);
2253                worker_sockets[thread_slot] = NULL;
2254            }
2255        }
2256
2257        /* If there are deferred lingering closes, handle them now. */ view japanese
2258        while (!workers_may_exit) {
2259            cs = defer_linger_chain;
2260            if (!cs) {
2261                break;
2262            }
2263            if (apr_atomic_casptr((void *)&defer_linger_chain, cs->chain,
2264                                  cs) != cs) {
2265                /* Race lost, try again */ view japanese
2266                continue;
2267            }
2268            cs->chain = NULL;
2269            AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(cs->pub.state == CONN_STATE_LINGER);
2270
2271            worker_sockets[thread_slot] = csd = cs->pfd.desc.s;
2272            process_socket(thd, cs->p, csd, cs, process_slot, thread_slot);
2273            worker_sockets[thread_slot] = NULL;
2274        }
2275    }
2276
2277    ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(process_slot, thread_slot,
2278                                        dying ? SERVER_DEAD
2279                                              : SERVER_GRACEFUL, NULL);
2280
2281    apr_thread_exit(thd, APR_SUCCESS);
2282    return NULL;
2283}
2284
2285static int check_signal(int signum)
2286{
2287    switch (signum) {
2288    case SIGTERM:
2289    case SIGINT:
2290        return 1;
2291    }
2292    return 0;
2293}
2294
2295static void create_listener_thread(thread_starter * ts)
2296{
2297    int my_child_num = ts->child_num_arg;
2298    apr_threadattr_t *thread_attr = ts->threadattr;
2299    proc_info *my_info;
2300    apr_status_t rv;
2301
2302    my_info = (proc_info *) ap_malloc(sizeof(proc_info));
2303    my_info->pslot = my_child_num;
2304    my_info->tslot = -1;      /* listener thread doesn't have a thread slot */ view japanese
2305    rv = ap_thread_create(&ts->listener, thread_attr, listener_thread,
2306                          my_info, pruntime);
2307    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2308        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00474)
2309                     "ap_thread_create: unable to create listener thread");
2310        /* let the parent decide how bad this really is */
2311        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDSICK);
2312    }
2313    apr_os_thread_get(&listener_os_thread, ts->listener);
2314}
2315
2316static void setup_threads_runtime(void)
2317{
2318    apr_status_t rv;
2319    ap_listen_rec *lr;
2320    apr_pool_t *pskip = NULL;
2321    int max_recycled_pools = -1, i;
2322    const int good_methods[] = { APR_POLLSET_KQUEUE,
2323                                 APR_POLLSET_PORT,
2324                                 APR_POLLSET_EPOLL };
2325    /* XXX: K-A or lingering close connection included in the async factor */ view japanese
2326    const apr_uint32_t async_factor = worker_factor / WORKER_FACTOR_SCALE;
2327    const apr_uint32_t pollset_size = (apr_uint32_t)num_listensocks +
2328                                      (apr_uint32_t)threads_per_child *
2329                                      (async_factor > 2 ? async_factor : 2);
2330    int pollset_flags;
2331
2332    /* Event's skiplist operations will happen concurrently with other modules'
2333     * runtime so they need their own pool for allocations, and its lifetime
2334     * should be at least the one of the connections (ptrans). Thus pskip is
2335     * created as a subpool of pconf like/before ptrans (before so that it's
2336     * destroyed after). In forked mode pconf is never destroyed so we are good
2337     * anyway, but in ONE_PROCESS mode this ensures that the skiplist works
2338     * from connection/ptrans cleanups (even after pchild is destroyed).
2339     */ view japanese
2340    apr_pool_create(&pskip, pconf);
2341    apr_pool_tag(pskip, "mpm_skiplist");
2342    apr_thread_mutex_create(&g_timer_skiplist_mtx, APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, pskip);
2343    APR_RING_INIT(&timer_free_ring.link, timer_event_t, link);
2344    apr_skiplist_init(&timer_skiplist, pskip);
2345    apr_skiplist_set_compare(timer_skiplist, timer_comp, timer_comp);
2346
2347    /* All threads (listener, workers) and synchronization objects (queues,
2348     * pollset, mutexes...) created here should have at least the lifetime of
2349     * the connections they handle (i.e. ptrans). We can't use this thread's
2350     * self pool because all these objects survive it, nor use pchild or pconf
2351     * directly because this starter thread races with other modules' runtime,
2352     * nor finally pchild (or subpool thereof) because it is killed explicitly
2353     * before pconf (thus connections/ptrans can live longer, which matters in
2354     * ONE_PROCESS mode). So this leaves us with a subpool of pconf, created
2355     * before any ptrans hence destroyed after.
2356     */ view japanese
2357    apr_pool_create(&pruntime, pconf);
2358    apr_pool_tag(pruntime, "mpm_runtime");
2359
2360    /* We must create the fd queues before we start up the listener
2361     * and worker threads. */ view japanese
2362    rv = ap_queue_create(&worker_queue, threads_per_child, pruntime);
2363    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2364        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03100)
2365                     "ap_queue_create() failed");
2366        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2367    }
2368
2369    if (ap_max_mem_free != APR_ALLOCATOR_MAX_FREE_UNLIMITED) {
2370        /* If we want to conserve memory, let's not keep an unlimited number of
2371         * pools & allocators.
2372         * XXX: This should probably be a separate config directive
2373         */ view japanese
2374        max_recycled_pools = threads_per_child * 3 / 4 ;
2375    }
2376    rv = ap_queue_info_create(&worker_queue_info, pruntime,
2377                              threads_per_child, max_recycled_pools);
2378    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2379        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03101)
2380                     "ap_queue_info_create() failed");
2381        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2382    }
2383
2384    /* Create the timeout mutex and main pollset before the listener
2385     * thread starts.
2386     */ view japanese
2387    rv = apr_thread_mutex_create(&timeout_mutex, APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT,
2388                                 pruntime);
2389    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2390        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03102)
2391                     "creation of the timeout mutex failed.");
2392        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2393    }
2394
2395    /* Create the main pollset. When APR_POLLSET_WAKEABLE is asked we account
2396     * for the wakeup pipe explicitely with pollset_size+1 because some pollset
2397     * implementations don't do it implicitely in APR.
2398     */ view japanese
2399    pollset_flags = APR_POLLSET_THREADSAFE | APR_POLLSET_NOCOPY |
2400                    APR_POLLSET_WAKEABLE | APR_POLLSET_NODEFAULT;
2401    for (i = 0; i < sizeof(good_methods) / sizeof(good_methods[0]); i++) {
2402        rv = apr_pollset_create_ex(&event_pollset, pollset_size + 1, pruntime,
2403                                   pollset_flags, good_methods[i]);
2404        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
2405            listener_is_wakeable = 1;
2406            break;
2407        }
2408    }
2409    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2410        pollset_flags &= ~APR_POLLSET_NODEFAULT;
2411        rv = apr_pollset_create(&event_pollset, pollset_size + 1, pruntime,
2412                                pollset_flags);
2413        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
2414            listener_is_wakeable = 1;
2415        }
2416        else {
2417            pollset_flags &= ~APR_POLLSET_WAKEABLE;
2418            rv = apr_pollset_create(&event_pollset, pollset_size, pruntime,
2419                                    pollset_flags);
2420        }
2421    }
2422    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2423        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03103)
2424                     "apr_pollset_create with Thread Safety failed.");
2425        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2426    }
2427
2428    /* Add listeners to the main pollset */ view japanese
2429    listener_pollfd = apr_pcalloc(pruntime, num_listensocks *
2430                                            sizeof(apr_pollfd_t));
2431    for (i = 0, lr = my_bucket->listeners; lr; lr = lr->next, i++) {
2432        apr_pollfd_t *pfd;
2433        listener_poll_type *pt;
2434
2435        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(i < num_listensocks);
2436        pfd = &listener_pollfd[i];
2437
2438        pfd->reqevents = APR_POLLIN | APR_POLLHUP | APR_POLLERR;
2439        pfd->desc_type = APR_POLL_SOCKET;
2440        pfd->desc.s = lr->sd;
2441
2442        pt = apr_pcalloc(pruntime, sizeof(*pt));
2443        pfd->client_data = pt;
2444        pt->type = PT_ACCEPT;
2445        pt->baton = lr;
2446
2447        apr_socket_opt_set(pfd->desc.s, APR_SO_NONBLOCK, 1);
2448        apr_pollset_add(event_pollset, pfd);
2449
2450        lr->accept_func = ap_unixd_accept;
2451    }
2452
2453    worker_sockets = apr_pcalloc(pruntime, threads_per_child *
2454                                           sizeof(apr_socket_t *));
2455}
2456
2457/* XXX under some circumstances not understood, children can get stuck
2458 *     in start_threads forever trying to take over slots which will
2459 *     never be cleaned up; for now there is an APLOG_DEBUG message issued
2460 *     every so often when this condition occurs
2461 */ view japanese
2462static void *APR_THREAD_FUNC start_threads(apr_thread_t * thd, void *dummy)
2463{
2464    thread_starter *ts = dummy;
2465    apr_thread_t **threads = ts->threads;
2466    apr_threadattr_t *thread_attr = ts->threadattr;
2467    int my_child_num = ts->child_num_arg;
2468    proc_info *my_info;
2469    apr_status_t rv;
2470    int threads_created = 0;
2471    int listener_started = 0;
2472    int prev_threads_created;
2473    int loops, i;
2474
2475    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02471)
2476                 "start_threads: Using %s (%swakeable)",
2477                 apr_pollset_method_name(event_pollset),
2478                 listener_is_wakeable ? "" : "not ");
2479
2480    loops = prev_threads_created = 0;
2481    while (1) {
2482        /* threads_per_child does not include the listener thread */ view japanese
2483        for (i = 0; i < threads_per_child; i++) {
2484            int status =
2485                ap_scoreboard_image->servers[my_child_num][i].status;
2486
2487            if (status != SERVER_DEAD) {
2488                continue;
2489            }
2490
2491            my_info = (proc_info *) ap_malloc(sizeof(proc_info));
2492            my_info->pslot = my_child_num;
2493            my_info->tslot = i;
2494
2495            /* We are creating threads right now */ view japanese
2496            ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(my_child_num, i,
2497                                                SERVER_STARTING, NULL);
2498            /* We let each thread update its own scoreboard entry.  This is
2499             * done because it lets us deal with tid better.
2500             */ view japanese
2501            rv = ap_thread_create(&threads[i], thread_attr,
2502                                  worker_thread, my_info, pruntime);
2503            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2504                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, rv, ap_server_conf,
2505                             APLOGNO(03104)
2506                             "ap_thread_create: unable to create worker thread");
2507                /* let the parent decide how bad this really is */
2508                clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDSICK);
2509            }
2510            threads_created++;
2511        }
2512
2513        /* Start the listener only when there are workers available */ view japanese
2514        if (!listener_started && threads_created) {
2515            create_listener_thread(ts);
2516            listener_started = 1;
2517        }
2518
2519
2520        if (start_thread_may_exit || threads_created == threads_per_child) {
2521            break;
2522        }
2523        /* wait for previous generation to clean up an entry */ view japanese
2524        apr_sleep(apr_time_from_sec(1));
2525        ++loops;
2526        if (loops % 120 == 0) { /* every couple of minutes */ view japanese
2527            if (prev_threads_created == threads_created) {
2528                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
2529                             "child %" APR_PID_T_FMT " isn't taking over "
2530                             "slots very quickly (%d of %d)",
2531                             ap_my_pid, threads_created,
2532                             threads_per_child);
2533            }
2534            prev_threads_created = threads_created;
2535        }
2536    }
2537
2538    /* What state should this child_main process be listed as in the
2539     * scoreboard...?
2540     *  ap_update_child_status_from_indexes(my_child_num, i, SERVER_STARTING,
2541     *                                      (request_rec *) NULL);
2542     *
2543     *  This state should be listed separately in the scoreboard, in some kind
2544     *  of process_status, not mixed in with the worker threads' status.
2545     *  "life_status" is almost right, but it's in the worker's structure, and
2546     *  the name could be clearer.   gla
2547     */ view japanese
2548    apr_thread_exit(thd, APR_SUCCESS);
2549    return NULL;
2550}
2551
2552static void join_workers(apr_thread_t * listener, apr_thread_t ** threads)
2553{
2554    int i;
2555    apr_status_t rv, thread_rv;
2556
2557    if (listener) {
2558        int iter;
2559
2560        /* deal with a rare timing window which affects waking up the
2561         * listener thread...  if the signal sent to the listener thread
2562         * is delivered between the time it verifies that the
2563         * listener_may_exit flag is clear and the time it enters a
2564         * blocking syscall, the signal didn't do any good...  work around
2565         * that by sleeping briefly and sending it again
2566         */ view japanese
2567
2568        iter = 0;
2569        while (!dying) {
2570            apr_sleep(apr_time_from_msec(500));
2571            if (dying || ++iter > 10) {
2572                break;
2573            }
2574            /* listener has not stopped accepting yet */ view japanese
2575            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
2576                         "listener has not stopped accepting yet (%d iter)", iter);
2577            wakeup_listener();
2578        }
2579        if (iter > 10) {
2580            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00475)
2581                         "the listener thread didn't stop accepting");
2582        }
2583        else {
2584            rv = apr_thread_join(&thread_rv, listener);
2585            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2586                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00476)
2587                             "apr_thread_join: unable to join listener thread");
2588            }
2589        }
2590    }
2591
2592    for (i = 0; i < threads_per_child; i++) {
2593        if (threads[i]) {       /* if we ever created this thread */ view japanese
2594            rv = apr_thread_join(&thread_rv, threads[i]);
2595            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2596                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00477)
2597                             "apr_thread_join: unable to join worker "
2598                             "thread %d", i);
2599            }
2600        }
2601    }
2602}
2603
2604static void join_start_thread(apr_thread_t * start_thread_id)
2605{
2606    apr_status_t rv, thread_rv;
2607
2608    start_thread_may_exit = 1;  /* tell it to give up in case it is still
2609                                 * trying to take over slots from a
2610                                 * previous generation
2611                                 */ view japanese
2612    rv = apr_thread_join(&thread_rv, start_thread_id);
2613    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2614        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00478)
2615                     "apr_thread_join: unable to join the start " "thread");
2616    }
2617}
2618
2619static void child_main(int child_num_arg, int child_bucket)
2620{
2621    apr_thread_t **threads;
2622    apr_status_t rv;
2623    thread_starter *ts;
2624    apr_threadattr_t *thread_attr;
2625    apr_thread_t *start_thread_id;
2626    int i;
2627
2628    /* for benefit of any hooks that run as this child initializes */ view japanese
2629    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STARTING;
2630
2631    ap_my_pid = getpid();
2632    ap_child_slot = child_num_arg;
2633    ap_fatal_signal_child_setup(ap_server_conf);
2634
2635    /* Get a sub context for global allocations in this child, so that
2636     * we can have cleanups occur when the child exits.
2637     */ view japanese
2638    apr_pool_create(&pchild, pconf);
2639    apr_pool_tag(pchild, "pchild");
2640
2641#if AP_HAS_THREAD_LOCAL
2642    if (!one_process) {
2643        apr_thread_t *thd = NULL;
2644        if ((rv = ap_thread_main_create(&thd, pchild))) {
2645            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_EMERG, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10377)
2646                         "Couldn't initialize child main thread");
2647            clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2648        }
2649    }
2650#endif
2651
2652    /* close unused listeners and pods */ view japanese
2653    for (i = 0; i < retained->mpm->num_buckets; i++) {
2654        if (i != child_bucket) {
2655            ap_close_listeners_ex(all_buckets[i].listeners);
2656            ap_mpm_podx_close(all_buckets[i].pod);
2657        }
2658    }
2659
2660    /*stuff to do before we switch id's, so we have permissions. */ view japanese
2661    ap_reopen_scoreboard(pchild, NULL, 0);
2662
2663    /* done with init critical section */ view japanese
2664    if (ap_run_drop_privileges(pchild, ap_server_conf)) {
2665        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2666    }
2667
2668    /* Just use the standard apr_setup_signal_thread to block all signals
2669     * from being received.  The child processes no longer use signals for
2670     * any communication with the parent process. Let's also do this before
2671     * child_init() hooks are called and possibly create threads that
2672     * otherwise could "steal" (implicitly) MPM's signals.
2673     */ view japanese
2674    rv = apr_setup_signal_thread();
2675    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2676        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_EMERG, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00479)
2677                     "Couldn't initialize signal thread");
2678        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDFATAL);
2679    }
2680
2681    ap_run_child_init(pchild, ap_server_conf);
2682
2683    if (ap_max_requests_per_child) {
2684        conns_this_child = ap_max_requests_per_child;
2685    }
2686    else {
2687        /* coding a value of zero means infinity */ view japanese
2688        conns_this_child = APR_INT32_MAX;
2689    }
2690
2691    /* Setup threads */ view japanese
2692
2693    /* Globals used by signal_threads() so to be initialized before */ view japanese
2694    setup_threads_runtime();
2695
2696    /* clear the storage; we may not create all our threads immediately,
2697     * and we want a 0 entry to indicate a thread which was not created
2698     */ view japanese
2699    threads = ap_calloc(threads_per_child, sizeof(apr_thread_t *));
2700    ts = apr_palloc(pchild, sizeof(*ts));
2701
2702    apr_threadattr_create(&thread_attr, pchild);
2703    /* 0 means PTHREAD_CREATE_JOINABLE */ view japanese
2704    apr_threadattr_detach_set(thread_attr, 0);
2705
2706    if (ap_thread_stacksize != 0) {
2707        rv = apr_threadattr_stacksize_set(thread_attr, ap_thread_stacksize);
2708        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && rv != APR_ENOTIMPL) {
2709            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02436)
2710                         "WARNING: ThreadStackSize of %" APR_SIZE_T_FMT " is "
2711                         "inappropriate, using default", 
2712                         ap_thread_stacksize);
2713        }
2714    }
2715
2716    ts->threads = threads;
2717    ts->listener = NULL;
2718    ts->child_num_arg = child_num_arg;
2719    ts->threadattr = thread_attr;
2720
2721    rv = ap_thread_create(&start_thread_id, thread_attr, start_threads,
2722                          ts, pchild);
2723    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2724        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00480)
2725                     "ap_thread_create: unable to create worker thread");
2726        /* let the parent decide how bad this really is */ view japanese
2727        clean_child_exit(APEXIT_CHILDSICK);
2728    }
2729
2730    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_RUNNING;
2731
2732    /* If we are only running in one_process mode, we will want to
2733     * still handle signals. */ view japanese
2734    if (one_process) {
2735        /* Block until we get a terminating signal. */ view japanese
2736        apr_signal_thread(check_signal);
2737        /* make sure the start thread has finished; signal_threads()
2738         * and join_workers() depend on that
2739         */ view japanese
2740        /* XXX join_start_thread() won't be awakened if one of our
2741         *     threads encounters a critical error and attempts to
2742         *     shutdown this child
2743         */ view japanese
2744        join_start_thread(start_thread_id);
2745
2746        /* helps us terminate a little more quickly than the dispatch of the
2747         * signal thread; beats the Pipe of Death and the browsers
2748         */ view japanese
2749        signal_threads(ST_UNGRACEFUL);
2750
2751        /* A terminating signal was received. Now join each of the
2752         * workers to clean them up.
2753         *   If the worker already exited, then the join frees
2754         *   their resources and returns.
2755         *   If the worker hasn't exited, then this blocks until
2756         *   they have (then cleans up).
2757         */
2758        join_workers(ts->listener, threads);
2759    }
2760    else {                      /* !one_process */ view japanese
2761        /* remove SIGTERM from the set of blocked signals...  if one of
2762         * the other threads in the process needs to take us down
2763         * (e.g., for MaxConnectionsPerChild) it will send us SIGTERM
2764         */ view japanese
2765        apr_signal(SIGTERM, dummy_signal_handler);
2766        unblock_signal(SIGTERM);
2767        /* Watch for any messages from the parent over the POD */ view japanese
2768        while (1) {
2769            rv = ap_mpm_podx_check(my_bucket->pod);
2770            if (rv == AP_MPM_PODX_NORESTART) {
2771                /* see if termination was triggered while we slept */ view japanese
2772                switch (terminate_mode) {
2773                case ST_GRACEFUL:
2774                    rv = AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL;
2775                    break;
2776                case ST_UNGRACEFUL:
2777                    rv = AP_MPM_PODX_RESTART;
2778                    break;
2779                }
2780            }
2781            if (rv == AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL || rv == AP_MPM_PODX_RESTART) {
2782                /* make sure the start thread has finished;
2783                 * signal_threads() and join_workers depend on that
2784                 */ view japanese
2785                join_start_thread(start_thread_id);
2786                signal_threads(rv ==
2787                               AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL ? ST_GRACEFUL : ST_UNGRACEFUL);
2788                break;
2789            }
2790        }
2791
2792        /* A terminating signal was received. Now join each of the
2793         * workers to clean them up.
2794         *   If the worker already exited, then the join frees
2795         *   their resources and returns.
2796         *   If the worker hasn't exited, then this blocks until
2797         *   they have (then cleans up).
2798         */ view japanese
2799        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
2800                     "%s termination received, joining workers",
2801                     rv == AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL ? "graceful" : "ungraceful");
2802        join_workers(ts->listener, threads);
2803        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
2804                     "%s termination, workers joined, exiting",
2805                     rv == AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL ? "graceful" : "ungraceful");
2806    }
2807
2808    free(threads);
2809
2810    clean_child_exit(resource_shortage ? APEXIT_CHILDSICK : 0);
2811}
2812
2813static int make_child(server_rec * s, int slot, int bucket)
2814{
2815    int pid;
2816
2817    if (slot + 1 > retained->max_daemon_used) {
2818        retained->max_daemon_used = slot + 1;
2819    }
2820
2821    if (ap_scoreboard_image->parent[slot].pid != 0) {
2822        /* XXX replace with assert or remove ? */ view japanese
2823        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03455)
2824                 "BUG: Scoreboard slot %d should be empty but is "
2825                 "in use by pid %" APR_PID_T_FMT,
2826                 slot, ap_scoreboard_image->parent[slot].pid);
2827        return -1;
2828    }
2829
2830    if (one_process) {
2831        my_bucket = &all_buckets[0];
2832
2833        event_note_child_started(slot, getpid());
2834        child_main(slot, 0);
2835        /* NOTREACHED */
2836        ap_assert(0);
2837        return -1;
2838    }
2839
2840    if ((pid = fork()) == -1) {
2841        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, errno, s, APLOGNO(00481)
2842                     "fork: Unable to fork new process");
2843
2844        /* fork didn't succeed.  There's no need to touch the scoreboard;
2845         * if we were trying to replace a failed child process, then
2846         * server_main_loop() marked its workers SERVER_DEAD, and if
2847         * we were trying to replace a child process that exited normally,
2848         * its worker_thread()s left SERVER_DEAD or SERVER_GRACEFUL behind.
2849         */ view japanese
2850
2851        /* In case system resources are maxxed out, we don't want
2852           Apache running away with the CPU trying to fork over and
2853           over and over again. */ view japanese
2854        apr_sleep(apr_time_from_sec(10));
2855
2856        return -1;
2857    }
2858
2859    if (!pid) {
2860#if AP_HAS_THREAD_LOCAL
2861        ap_thread_current_after_fork();
2862#endif
2863
2864        my_bucket = &all_buckets[bucket];
2865
2866#ifdef HAVE_BINDPROCESSOR
2867        /* By default, AIX binds to a single processor.  This bit unbinds
2868         * children which will then bind to another CPU.
2869         */ view japanese
2870        int status = bindprocessor(BINDPROCESS, (int) getpid(),
2871                                   PROCESSOR_CLASS_ANY);
2872        if (status != OK)
2873            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, errno,
2874                         ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00482)
2875                         "processor unbind failed");
2876#endif
2877        RAISE_SIGSTOP(MAKE_CHILD);
2878
2879        apr_signal(SIGTERM, just_die);
2880        child_main(slot, bucket);
2881        /* NOTREACHED */ view japanese
2882        ap_assert(0);
2883        return -1;
2884    }
2885
2886    event_note_child_started(slot, pid);
2887    return 0;
2888}
2889
2890/* start up a bunch of children */ view japanese
2891static void startup_children(int number_to_start)
2892{
2893    int i;
2894
2895    for (i = 0; number_to_start && i < server_limit; ++i) {
2896        if (ap_scoreboard_image->parent[i].pid != 0) {
2897            continue;
2898        }
2899        if (make_child(ap_server_conf, i, i % retained->mpm->num_buckets) < 0) {
2900            break;
2901        }
2902        --number_to_start;
2903    }
2904}
2905
2906static void perform_idle_server_maintenance(int child_bucket,
2907                                            int *max_daemon_used)
2908{
2909    int num_buckets = retained->mpm->num_buckets;
2910    int idle_thread_count = 0;
2911    process_score *ps;
2912    int free_length = 0;
2913    int free_slots[MAX_SPAWN_RATE];
2914    int last_non_dead = -1;
2915    int active_thread_count = 0;
2916    int i, j;
2917
2918    for (i = 0; i < server_limit; ++i) {
2919        if (num_buckets > 1 && (i % num_buckets) != child_bucket) {
2920            /* We only care about child_bucket in this call */ view japanese
2921            continue;
2922        }
2923        if (i >= retained->max_daemon_used &&
2924            free_length == retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket]) {
2925            /* short cut if all active processes have been examined and
2926             * enough empty scoreboard slots have been found
2927             */ view japanese
2928            break;
2929        }
2930
2931        ps = &ap_scoreboard_image->parent[i];
2932        if (ps->pid != 0) {
2933            int child_threads_active = 0;
2934            if (ps->quiescing == 1) {
2935                ps->quiescing = 2;
2936                retained->active_daemons--;
2937                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf,
2938                             "Child %d quiescing: pid %d, gen %d, "
2939                             "active %d/%d, total %d/%d/%d",
2940                             i, (int)ps->pid, (int)ps->generation,
2941                             retained->active_daemons, active_daemons_limit,
2942                             retained->total_daemons, retained->max_daemon_used,
2943                             server_limit);
2944            }
2945            for (j = 0; j < threads_per_child; j++) {
2946                int status = ap_scoreboard_image->servers[i][j].status;
2947
2948                /* We consider a starting server as idle because we started it
2949                 * at least a cycle ago, and if it still hasn't finished starting
2950                 * then we're just going to swamp things worse by forking more.
2951                 * So we hopefully won't need to fork more if we count it.
2952                 * This depends on the ordering of SERVER_READY and SERVER_STARTING.
2953                 */ view japanese
2954                if (status <= SERVER_READY && !ps->quiescing && !ps->not_accepting
2955                    && ps->generation == retained->mpm->my_generation) {
2956                    ++idle_thread_count;
2957                }
2958                if (status >= SERVER_READY && status < SERVER_GRACEFUL) {
2959                    ++child_threads_active;
2960                }
2961            }
2962            active_thread_count += child_threads_active;
2963            if (child_threads_active == threads_per_child) {
2964                had_healthy_child = 1;
2965            }
2966            last_non_dead = i;
2967        }
2968        else if (free_length < retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket]) {
2969            free_slots[free_length++] = i;
2970        }
2971    }
2972    if (*max_daemon_used < last_non_dead + 1) {
2973        *max_daemon_used = last_non_dead + 1;
2974    }
2975
2976    if (retained->sick_child_detected) {
2977        if (had_healthy_child) {
2978            /* Assume this is a transient error, even though it may not be.  Leave
2979             * the server up in case it is able to serve some requests or the
2980             * problem will be resolved.
2981             */ view japanese
2982            retained->sick_child_detected = 0;
2983        }
2984        else if (child_bucket < num_buckets - 1) {
2985            /* check for had_healthy_child up to the last child bucket */ view japanese
2986            return;
2987        }
2988        else {
2989            /* looks like a basket case, as no child ever fully initialized; give up.
2990             */ view japanese
2991            retained->mpm->shutdown_pending = 1;
2992            child_fatal = 1;
2993            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT, 0,
2994                         ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02324)
2995                         "A resource shortage or other unrecoverable failure "
2996                         "was encountered before any child process initialized "
2997                         "successfully... httpd is exiting!");
2998            /* the child already logged the failure details */ view japanese
2999            return;
3000        }
3001    }
3002
3003    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(retained->active_daemons <= retained->total_daemons
3004                    && retained->total_daemons <= retained->max_daemon_used
3005                    && retained->max_daemon_used <= server_limit);
3006
3007    if (idle_thread_count > max_spare_threads / num_buckets) {
3008        /*
3009         * Child processes that we ask to shut down won't die immediately
3010         * but may stay around for a long time when they finish their
3011         * requests. If the server load changes many times, many such
3012         * gracefully finishing processes may accumulate, filling up the
3013         * scoreboard. To avoid running out of scoreboard entries, we
3014         * don't shut down more processes if there are stopping ones
3015         * already (i.e. active_daemons != total_daemons) and not enough
3016         * slack space in the scoreboard for a graceful restart.
3017         *
3018         * XXX It would be nice if we could
3019         * XXX - kill processes without keepalive connections first
3020         * XXX - tell children to stop accepting new connections, and
3021         * XXX   depending on server load, later be able to resurrect them
3022         *       or kill them
3023         */ view japanese
3024        int do_kill = (retained->active_daemons == retained->total_daemons
3025                       || (server_limit - retained->total_daemons >
3026                           active_daemons_limit));
3027        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE5, 0, ap_server_conf,
3028                     "%shutting down one child: "
3029                     "active %d/%d, total %d/%d/%d, "
3030                     "idle threads %d, max workers %d",
3031                     (do_kill) ? "S" : "Not s",
3032                     retained->active_daemons, active_daemons_limit,
3033                     retained->total_daemons, retained->max_daemon_used,
3034                     server_limit, idle_thread_count, max_workers);
3035        if (do_kill) {
3036            ap_mpm_podx_signal(all_buckets[child_bucket].pod,
3037                               AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL);
3038        }
3039        else {
3040            /* Wait for dying daemon(s) to exit */ view japanese
3041        }
3042        retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] = 1;
3043    }
3044    else if (idle_thread_count < min_spare_threads / num_buckets) {
3045        if (active_thread_count >= max_workers / num_buckets) {
3046            if (0 == idle_thread_count) { 
3047                if (!retained->maxclients_reported) {
3048                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00484)
3049                                 "server reached MaxRequestWorkers setting, "
3050                                 "consider raising the MaxRequestWorkers "
3051                                 "setting");
3052                    retained->maxclients_reported = 1;
3053                }
3054             }
3055             else { 
3056                if (!retained->near_maxclients_reported) {
3057                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10159)
3058                            "server is within MinSpareThreads of "
3059                            "MaxRequestWorkers, consider raising the "
3060                            "MaxRequestWorkers setting");
3061                    retained->near_maxclients_reported = 1;
3062                }
3063            }
3064            retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] = 1;
3065        }
3066        else if (free_length == 0) { /* scoreboard is full, can't fork */ view japanese
3067            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(03490)
3068                         "scoreboard is full, not at MaxRequestWorkers."
3069                         "Increase ServerLimit.");
3070            retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] = 1;
3071        }
3072        else {
3073            if (free_length > retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket]) {
3074                free_length = retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket];
3075            }
3076            if (free_length + retained->active_daemons > active_daemons_limit) {
3077                if (retained->active_daemons < active_daemons_limit) {
3078                    free_length = active_daemons_limit - retained->active_daemons;
3079                }
3080                else {
3081                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, ap_server_conf,
3082                                 "server is at active daemons limit, spawning "
3083                                 "of %d children cancelled: active %d/%d, "
3084                                 "total %d/%d/%d, rate %d", free_length,
3085                                 retained->active_daemons, active_daemons_limit,
3086                                 retained->total_daemons, retained->max_daemon_used,
3087                                 server_limit, retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket]);
3088                    /* reset the spawning rate and prevent its growth below */ view japanese
3089                    retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] = 1;
3090                    ++retained->hold_off_on_exponential_spawning;
3091                    free_length = 0;
3092                }
3093            }
3094            if (retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] >= 8) {
3095                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00486)
3096                             "server seems busy, (you may need "
3097                             "to increase StartServers, ThreadsPerChild "
3098                             "or Min/MaxSpareThreads), "
3099                             "spawning %d children, there are around %d idle "
3100                             "threads, %d active children, and %d children "
3101                             "that are shutting down", free_length,
3102                             idle_thread_count, retained->active_daemons,
3103                             retained->total_daemons);
3104            }
3105            for (i = 0; i < free_length; ++i) {
3106                int slot = free_slots[i];
3107                if (make_child(ap_server_conf, slot, child_bucket) < 0) {
3108                    continue;
3109                }
3110                if (*max_daemon_used < slot + 1) {
3111                    *max_daemon_used = slot + 1;
3112                }
3113            }
3114            /* the next time around we want to spawn twice as many if this
3115             * wasn't good enough, but not if we've just done a graceful
3116             */ view japanese
3117            if (retained->hold_off_on_exponential_spawning) {
3118                --retained->hold_off_on_exponential_spawning;
3119            }
3120            else if (retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket]
3121                     < MAX_SPAWN_RATE / num_buckets) {
3122                retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] *= 2;
3123            }
3124        }
3125    }
3126    else {
3127        retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_bucket] = 1;
3128    }
3129}
3130
3131static void server_main_loop(int remaining_children_to_start)
3132{
3133    int num_buckets = retained->mpm->num_buckets;
3134    int max_daemon_used = 0;
3135    int successive_kills = 0;
3136    int child_slot;
3137    apr_exit_why_e exitwhy;
3138    int status, processed_status;
3139    apr_proc_t pid;
3140    int i;
3141
3142    while (!retained->mpm->restart_pending && !retained->mpm->shutdown_pending) {
3143        ap_wait_or_timeout(&exitwhy, &status, &pid, pconf, ap_server_conf);
3144
3145        if (pid.pid != -1) {
3146            processed_status = ap_process_child_status(&pid, exitwhy, status);
3147            child_slot = ap_find_child_by_pid(&pid);
3148            if (processed_status == APEXIT_CHILDFATAL) {
3149                /* fix race condition found in PR 39311
3150                 * A child created at the same time as a graceful happens 
3151                 * can find the lock missing and create a fatal error.
3152                 * It is not fatal for the last generation to be in this state.
3153                 */ view japanese
3154                if (child_slot < 0
3155                    || ap_get_scoreboard_process(child_slot)->generation
3156                       == retained->mpm->my_generation) {
3157                    retained->mpm->shutdown_pending = 1;
3158                    child_fatal = 1;
3159                    /*
3160                     * total_daemons counting will be off now, but as we
3161                     * are shutting down, that is not an issue anymore.
3162                     */ view japanese
3163                    return;
3164                }
3165                else {
3166                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00487)
3167                                 "Ignoring fatal error in child of previous "
3168                                 "generation (pid %ld).",
3169                                 (long)pid.pid);
3170                    retained->sick_child_detected = 1;
3171                }
3172            }
3173            else if (processed_status == APEXIT_CHILDSICK) {
3174                /* tell perform_idle_server_maintenance to check into this
3175                 * on the next timer pop
3176                 */ view japanese
3177                retained->sick_child_detected = 1;
3178            }
3179            /* non-fatal death... note that it's gone in the scoreboard. */ view japanese
3180            if (child_slot >= 0) {
3181                event_note_child_stopped(child_slot, 0, 0);
3182
3183                if (processed_status == APEXIT_CHILDSICK) {
3184                    /* resource shortage, minimize the fork rate */ view japanese
3185                    retained->idle_spawn_rate[child_slot % num_buckets] = 1;
3186                }
3187                else if (remaining_children_to_start) {
3188                    /* we're still doing a 1-for-1 replacement of dead
3189                     * children with new children
3190                     */ view japanese
3191                    make_child(ap_server_conf, child_slot,
3192                               child_slot % num_buckets);
3193                    --remaining_children_to_start;
3194                }
3195            }
3196#if APR_HAS_OTHER_CHILD
3197            else if (apr_proc_other_child_alert(&pid, APR_OC_REASON_DEATH,
3198                                                status) == 0) {
3199                /* handled */ view japanese
3200            }
3201#endif
3202            else if (retained->mpm->was_graceful) {
3203                /* Great, we've probably just lost a slot in the
3204                 * scoreboard.  Somehow we don't know about this child.
3205                 */ view japanese
3206                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0,
3207                             ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00488)
3208                             "long lost child came home! (pid %ld)",
3209                             (long) pid.pid);
3210            }
3211            /* Don't perform idle maintenance when a child dies,
3212             * only do it when there's a timeout.  Remember only a
3213             * finite number of children can die, and it's pretty
3214             * pathological for a lot to die suddenly.  If a child is
3215             * killed by a signal (faulting) we want to restart it ASAP
3216             * though, up to 3 successive faults or we stop this until
3217             * a timeout happens again (to avoid the flood of fork()ed
3218             * processes that keep being killed early).
3219             */ view japanese
3220            if (child_slot < 0 || !APR_PROC_CHECK_SIGNALED(exitwhy)) {
3221                continue;
3222            }
3223            if (++successive_kills >= 3) {
3224                if (successive_kills % 10 == 3) {
3225                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0,
3226                                 ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10392)
3227                                 "children are killed successively!");
3228                }
3229                continue;
3230            }
3231            ++remaining_children_to_start;
3232        }
3233        else {
3234            successive_kills = 0;
3235        }
3236
3237        if (remaining_children_to_start) {
3238            /* we hit a 1 second timeout in which none of the previous
3239             * generation of children needed to be reaped... so assume
3240             * they're all done, and pick up the slack if any is left.
3241             */ view japanese
3242            startup_children(remaining_children_to_start);
3243            remaining_children_to_start = 0;
3244            /* In any event we really shouldn't do the code below because
3245             * few of the servers we just started are in the IDLE state
3246             * yet, so we'd mistakenly create an extra server.
3247             */ view japanese
3248            continue;
3249        }
3250
3251        max_daemon_used = 0;
3252        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3253            perform_idle_server_maintenance(i, &max_daemon_used);
3254        }
3255        retained->max_daemon_used = max_daemon_used;
3256    }
3257}
3258
3259static int event_run(apr_pool_t * _pconf, apr_pool_t * plog, server_rec * s)
3260{
3261    int num_buckets = retained->mpm->num_buckets;
3262    int remaining_children_to_start;
3263    int i;
3264
3265    ap_log_pid(pconf, ap_pid_fname);
3266
3267    if (!retained->mpm->was_graceful) {
3268        if (ap_run_pre_mpm(s->process->pool, SB_SHARED) != OK) {
3269            retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STOPPING;
3270            return !OK;
3271        }
3272        /* fix the generation number in the global score; we just got a new,
3273         * cleared scoreboard
3274         */ view japanese
3275        ap_scoreboard_image->global->running_generation = retained->mpm->my_generation;
3276    }
3277
3278    ap_unixd_mpm_set_signals(pconf, one_process);
3279
3280    /* Don't thrash since num_buckets depends on the
3281     * system and the number of online CPU cores...
3282     */ view japanese
3283    if (active_daemons_limit < num_buckets)
3284        active_daemons_limit = num_buckets;
3285    if (ap_daemons_to_start < num_buckets)
3286        ap_daemons_to_start = num_buckets;
3287    /* We want to create as much children at a time as the number of buckets,
3288     * so to optimally accept connections (evenly distributed across buckets).
3289     * Thus min_spare_threads should at least maintain num_buckets children,
3290     * and max_spare_threads allow num_buckets more children w/o triggering
3291     * immediately (e.g. num_buckets idle threads margin, one per bucket).
3292     */ view japanese
3293    if (min_spare_threads < threads_per_child * (num_buckets - 1) + num_buckets)
3294        min_spare_threads = threads_per_child * (num_buckets - 1) + num_buckets;
3295    if (max_spare_threads < min_spare_threads + (threads_per_child + 1) * num_buckets)
3296        max_spare_threads = min_spare_threads + (threads_per_child + 1) * num_buckets;
3297
3298    /* If we're doing a graceful_restart then we're going to see a lot
3299     * of children exiting immediately when we get into the main loop
3300     * below (because we just sent them AP_SIG_GRACEFUL).  This happens pretty
3301     * rapidly... and for each one that exits we may start a new one, until
3302     * there are at least min_spare_threads idle threads, counting across
3303     * all children.  But we may be permitted to start more children than
3304     * that, so we'll just keep track of how many we're
3305     * supposed to start up without the 1 second penalty between each fork.
3306     */ view japanese
3307    remaining_children_to_start = ap_daemons_to_start;
3308    if (remaining_children_to_start > active_daemons_limit) {
3309        remaining_children_to_start = active_daemons_limit;
3310    }
3311    if (!retained->mpm->was_graceful) {
3312        startup_children(remaining_children_to_start);
3313        remaining_children_to_start = 0;
3314    }
3315    else {
3316        /* give the system some time to recover before kicking into
3317         * exponential mode */ view japanese
3318        retained->hold_off_on_exponential_spawning = 10;
3319    }
3320
3321    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_NOTICE, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00489)
3322                 "%s configured -- resuming normal operations",
3323                 ap_get_server_description());
3324    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00490)
3325                 "Server built: %s", ap_get_server_built());
3326    ap_log_command_line(plog, s);
3327    ap_log_mpm_common(s);
3328
3329    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_RUNNING;
3330
3331    server_main_loop(remaining_children_to_start);
3332    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STOPPING;
3333
3334    if (retained->mpm->shutdown_pending && retained->mpm->is_ungraceful) {
3335        /* Time to shut down:
3336         * Kill child processes, tell them to call child_exit, etc...
3337         */ view japanese
3338        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3339            ap_mpm_podx_killpg(all_buckets[i].pod, active_daemons_limit,
3340                               AP_MPM_PODX_RESTART);
3341        }
3342        ap_reclaim_child_processes(1, /* Start with SIGTERM */
3343                                   event_note_child_stopped);
3344
3345        if (!child_fatal) {
3346            /* cleanup pid file on normal shutdown */
3347            ap_remove_pid(pconf, ap_pid_fname);
3348            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_NOTICE, 0,
3349                         ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00491) "caught SIGTERM, shutting down");
3350        }
3351
3352        return DONE;
3353    }
3354
3355    if (retained->mpm->shutdown_pending) {
3356        /* Time to gracefully shut down:
3357         * Kill child processes, tell them to call child_exit, etc...
3358         */ view japanese
3359        int active_children;
3360        int index;
3361        apr_time_t cutoff = 0;
3362
3363        /* Close our listeners, and then ask our children to do same */ view japanese
3364        ap_close_listeners();
3365        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3366            ap_mpm_podx_killpg(all_buckets[i].pod, active_daemons_limit,
3367                               AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL);
3368        }
3369        ap_relieve_child_processes(event_note_child_stopped);
3370
3371        if (!child_fatal) {
3372            /* cleanup pid file on normal shutdown */ view japanese
3373            ap_remove_pid(pconf, ap_pid_fname);
3374            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_NOTICE, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00492)
3375                         "caught " AP_SIG_GRACEFUL_STOP_STRING
3376                         ", shutting down gracefully");
3377        }
3378
3379        if (ap_graceful_shutdown_timeout) {
3380            cutoff = apr_time_now() +
3381                     apr_time_from_sec(ap_graceful_shutdown_timeout);
3382        }
3383
3384        /* Don't really exit until each child has finished */ view japanese
3385        retained->mpm->shutdown_pending = 0;
3386        do {
3387            /* Pause for a second */ view japanese
3388            apr_sleep(apr_time_from_sec(1));
3389
3390            /* Relieve any children which have now exited */ view japanese
3391            ap_relieve_child_processes(event_note_child_stopped);
3392
3393            active_children = 0;
3394            for (index = 0; index < retained->max_daemon_used; ++index) {
3395                if (ap_mpm_safe_kill(MPM_CHILD_PID(index), 0) == APR_SUCCESS) {
3396                    active_children = 1;
3397                    /* Having just one child is enough to stay around */ view japanese
3398                    break;
3399                }
3400            }
3401        } while (!retained->mpm->shutdown_pending && active_children &&
3402                 (!ap_graceful_shutdown_timeout || apr_time_now() < cutoff));
3403
3404        /* We might be here because we received SIGTERM, either
3405         * way, try and make sure that all of our processes are
3406         * really dead.
3407         */ view japanese
3408        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3409            ap_mpm_podx_killpg(all_buckets[i].pod, active_daemons_limit,
3410                               AP_MPM_PODX_RESTART);
3411        }
3412        ap_reclaim_child_processes(1, event_note_child_stopped);
3413
3414        return DONE;
3415    }
3416
3417    /* we've been told to restart */ view japanese
3418    if (one_process) {
3419        /* not worth thinking about */ view japanese
3420        return DONE;
3421    }
3422
3423    /* advance to the next generation */ view japanese
3424    /* XXX: we really need to make sure this new generation number isn't in
3425     * use by any of the children.
3426     */ view japanese
3427    ++retained->mpm->my_generation;
3428    ap_scoreboard_image->global->running_generation = retained->mpm->my_generation;
3429
3430    if (!retained->mpm->is_ungraceful) {
3431        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_NOTICE, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00493)
3432                     AP_SIG_GRACEFUL_STRING " received.  Doing graceful restart");
3433        /* wake up the children...time to die.  But we'll have more soon */ view japanese
3434        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3435            ap_mpm_podx_killpg(all_buckets[i].pod, active_daemons_limit,
3436                               AP_MPM_PODX_GRACEFUL);
3437        }
3438
3439        /* This is mostly for debugging... so that we know what is still
3440         * gracefully dealing with existing request.
3441         */ view japanese
3442
3443    }
3444    else {
3445        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_NOTICE, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(00494)
3446                     "SIGHUP received.  Attempting to restart");
3447        /* Kill 'em all.  Since the child acts the same on the parents SIGTERM
3448         * and a SIGHUP, we may as well use the same signal, because some user
3449         * pthreads are stealing signals from us left and right.
3450         */ view japanese
3451        for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3452            ap_mpm_podx_killpg(all_buckets[i].pod, active_daemons_limit,
3453                               AP_MPM_PODX_RESTART);
3454        }
3455
3456        ap_reclaim_child_processes(1,  /* Start with SIGTERM */ view japanese
3457                                   event_note_child_stopped);
3458    }
3459
3460    return OK;
3461}
3462
3463static void setup_slave_conn(conn_rec *c, void *csd) 
3464{
3465    event_conn_state_t *mcs;
3466    event_conn_state_t *cs;
3467    
3468    mcs = ap_get_module_config(c->master->conn_config, &mpm_event_module);
3469    
3470    cs = apr_pcalloc(c->pool, sizeof(*cs));
3471    cs->c = c;
3472    cs->r = NULL;
3473    cs->sc = mcs->sc;
3474    cs->suspended = 0;
3475    cs->p = c->pool;
3476    cs->bucket_alloc = c->bucket_alloc;
3477    cs->pfd = mcs->pfd;
3478    cs->pub = mcs->pub;
3479    cs->pub.state = CONN_STATE_PROCESSING;
3480    cs->pub.sense = CONN_SENSE_DEFAULT;
3481    
3482    c->cs = &(cs->pub);
3483    ap_set_module_config(c->conn_config, &mpm_event_module, cs);
3484}
3485
3486static int event_pre_connection(conn_rec *c, void *csd)
3487{
3488    if (c->master && (!c->cs || c->cs == c->master->cs)) {
3489        setup_slave_conn(c, csd);
3490    }
3491    return OK;
3492}
3493
3494static int event_protocol_switch(conn_rec *c, request_rec *r, server_rec *s,
3495                                 const char *protocol)
3496{
3497    if (!r && s) {
3498        /* connection based switching of protocol, set the correct server
3499         * configuration, so that timeouts, keepalives and such are used
3500         * for the server that the connection was switched on.
3501         * Normally, we set this on post_read_request, but on a protocol
3502         * other than http/1.1, this might never happen.
3503         */ view japanese
3504        event_conn_state_t *cs;
3505        
3506        cs = ap_get_module_config(c->conn_config, &mpm_event_module);
3507        cs->sc = ap_get_module_config(s->module_config, &mpm_event_module);
3508    }
3509    return DECLINED;
3510}
3511
3512/* This really should be a post_config hook, but the error log is already
3513 * redirected by that point, so we need to do this in the open_logs phase.
3514 */ view japanese
3515static int event_open_logs(apr_pool_t * p, apr_pool_t * plog,
3516                           apr_pool_t * ptemp, server_rec * s)
3517{
3518    int startup = 0;
3519    int level_flags = 0;
3520    int num_buckets = 0;
3521    ap_listen_rec **listen_buckets;
3522    apr_status_t rv;
3523    int i;
3524
3525    pconf = p;
3526
3527    /* the reverse of pre_config, we want this only the first time around */
3528    if (retained->mpm->module_loads == 1) {
3529        startup = 1;
3530        level_flags |= APLOG_STARTUP;
3531    }
3532
3533    if ((num_listensocks = ap_setup_listeners(ap_server_conf)) < 1) {
3534        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ALERT | level_flags, 0,
3535                     (startup ? NULL : s),
3536                     "no listening sockets available, shutting down");
3537        return !OK;
3538    }
3539
3540    if (one_process) {
3541        num_buckets = 1;
3542    }
3543    else if (retained->mpm->was_graceful) {
3544        /* Preserve the number of buckets on graceful restarts. */ view japanese
3545        num_buckets = retained->mpm->num_buckets;
3546    }
3547    if ((rv = ap_duplicate_listeners(pconf, ap_server_conf,
3548                                     &listen_buckets, &num_buckets))) {
3549        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT | level_flags, rv,
3550                     (startup ? NULL : s),
3551                     "could not duplicate listeners");
3552        return !OK;
3553    }
3554
3555    all_buckets = apr_pcalloc(pconf, num_buckets * sizeof(*all_buckets));
3556    for (i = 0; i < num_buckets; i++) {
3557        if (!one_process && /* no POD in one_process mode */ view japanese
3558                (rv = ap_mpm_podx_open(pconf, &all_buckets[i].pod))) {
3559            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT | level_flags, rv,
3560                         (startup ? NULL : s),
3561                         "could not open pipe-of-death");
3562            return !OK;
3563        }
3564        all_buckets[i].listeners = listen_buckets[i];
3565    }
3566
3567    if (retained->mpm->max_buckets < num_buckets) {
3568        int new_max, *new_ptr;
3569        new_max = retained->mpm->max_buckets * 2;
3570        if (new_max < num_buckets) {
3571            new_max = num_buckets;
3572        }
3573        new_ptr = (int *)apr_palloc(ap_pglobal, new_max * sizeof(int));
3574        if (retained->idle_spawn_rate) /* NULL at startup */ view japanese
3575            memcpy(new_ptr, retained->idle_spawn_rate,
3576                   retained->mpm->num_buckets * sizeof(int));
3577        retained->idle_spawn_rate = new_ptr;
3578        retained->mpm->max_buckets = new_max;
3579    }
3580    if (retained->mpm->num_buckets < num_buckets) {
3581        int rate_max = 1;
3582        /* If new buckets are added, set their idle spawn rate to
3583         * the highest so far, so that they get filled as quickly
3584         * as the existing ones.
3585         */ view japanese
3586        for (i = 0; i < retained->mpm->num_buckets; i++) {
3587            if (rate_max < retained->idle_spawn_rate[i]) {
3588                rate_max = retained->idle_spawn_rate[i];
3589            }
3590        }
3591        for (/* up to date i */; i < num_buckets; i++) { view japanese
3592            retained->idle_spawn_rate[i] = rate_max;
3593        }
3594    }
3595    retained->mpm->num_buckets = num_buckets;
3596
3597    /* for skiplist */ view japanese
3598    srand((unsigned int)apr_time_now());
3599    return OK;
3600}
3601
3602static int event_pre_config(apr_pool_t * pconf, apr_pool_t * plog,
3603                            apr_pool_t * ptemp)
3604{
3605    int no_detach, debug, foreground;
3606    apr_status_t rv;
3607    const char *userdata_key = "mpm_event_module";
3608    int test_atomics = 0;
3609
3610    debug = ap_exists_config_define("DEBUG");
3611
3612    if (debug) {
3613        foreground = one_process = 1;
3614        no_detach = 0;
3615    }
3616    else {
3617        one_process = ap_exists_config_define("ONE_PROCESS");
3618        no_detach = ap_exists_config_define("NO_DETACH");
3619        foreground = ap_exists_config_define("FOREGROUND");
3620    }
3621
3622    retained = ap_retained_data_get(userdata_key);
3623    if (!retained) {
3624        retained = ap_retained_data_create(userdata_key, sizeof(*retained));
3625        retained->mpm = ap_unixd_mpm_get_retained_data();
3626        if (retained->mpm->module_loads) {
3627            test_atomics = 1;
3628        }
3629    }
3630    retained->mpm->mpm_state = AP_MPMQ_STARTING;
3631    if (retained->mpm->baton != retained) {
3632        retained->mpm->was_graceful = 0;
3633        retained->mpm->baton = retained;
3634    }
3635    ++retained->mpm->module_loads;
3636
3637    /* test once for correct operation of fdqueue */ view japanese
3638    if (test_atomics || retained->mpm->module_loads == 2) {
3639        static apr_uint32_t foo1, foo2;
3640
3641        apr_atomic_set32(&foo1, 100);
3642        foo2 = apr_atomic_add32(&foo1, -10);
3643        if (foo2 != 100 || foo1 != 90) {
3644            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(02405)
3645                         "atomics not working as expected - add32 of negative number");
3646            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
3647        }
3648    }
3649
3650    /* sigh, want this only the second time around */ view japanese
3651    if (retained->mpm->module_loads == 2) {
3652        rv = apr_pollset_create(&event_pollset, 1, plog,
3653                                APR_POLLSET_THREADSAFE | APR_POLLSET_NOCOPY);
3654        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3655            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, NULL, APLOGNO(00495)
3656                         "Couldn't create a Thread Safe Pollset. "
3657                         "Is it supported on your platform?"
3658                         "Also check system or user limits!");
3659            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
3660        }
3661        apr_pollset_destroy(event_pollset);
3662
3663        if (!one_process && !foreground) {
3664            /* before we detach, setup crash handlers to log to errorlog */ view japanese
3665            ap_fatal_signal_setup(ap_server_conf, pconf);
3666            rv = apr_proc_detach(no_detach ? APR_PROC_DETACH_FOREGROUND
3667                                 : APR_PROC_DETACH_DAEMONIZE);
3668            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3669                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, NULL, APLOGNO(00496)
3670                             "apr_proc_detach failed");
3671                return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
3672            }
3673        }
3674    }
3675
3676    parent_pid = ap_my_pid = getpid();
3677
3678    ap_listen_pre_config();
3679    ap_daemons_to_start = DEFAULT_START_DAEMON;
3680    min_spare_threads = DEFAULT_MIN_FREE_DAEMON * DEFAULT_THREADS_PER_CHILD;
3681    max_spare_threads = DEFAULT_MAX_FREE_DAEMON * DEFAULT_THREADS_PER_CHILD;
3682    server_limit = DEFAULT_SERVER_LIMIT;
3683    thread_limit = DEFAULT_THREAD_LIMIT;
3684    active_daemons_limit = server_limit;
3685    threads_per_child = DEFAULT_THREADS_PER_CHILD;
3686    max_workers = active_daemons_limit * threads_per_child;
3687    defer_linger_chain = NULL;
3688    had_healthy_child = 0;
3689    ap_extended_status = 0;
3690
3691    event_pollset = NULL;
3692    worker_queue_info = NULL;
3693    listener_os_thread = NULL;
3694    listensocks_disabled = 0;
3695    listener_is_wakeable = 0;
3696
3697    return OK;
3698}
3699
3700static int event_post_config(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog,
3701                             apr_pool_t *ptemp, server_rec *s)
3702{
3703    struct {
3704        struct timeout_queue *tail, *q;
3705        apr_hash_t *hash;
3706    } io, wc, ka;
3707
3708    /* Not needed in pre_config stage */ view japanese
3709    if (ap_state_query(AP_SQ_MAIN_STATE) == AP_SQ_MS_CREATE_PRE_CONFIG) {
3710        return OK;
3711    }
3712
3713    io.hash = apr_hash_make(ptemp);
3714    wc.hash = apr_hash_make(ptemp);
3715    ka.hash = apr_hash_make(ptemp);
3716    io.tail = wc.tail = ka.tail = NULL;
3717
3718    linger_q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, apr_time_from_sec(MAX_SECS_TO_LINGER),
3719                             NULL);
3720    short_linger_q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, apr_time_from_sec(SECONDS_TO_LINGER),
3721                                   NULL);
3722
3723    for (; s; s = s->next) {
3724        event_srv_cfg *sc = apr_pcalloc(pconf, sizeof *sc);
3725
3726        ap_set_module_config(s->module_config, &mpm_event_module, sc);
3727        if (!io.tail) {
3728            /* The main server uses the global queues */ view japanese
3729            io.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->timeout, NULL);
3730            apr_hash_set(io.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout, io.q);
3731            io.tail = waitio_q = io.q;
3732
3733            wc.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->timeout, NULL);
3734            apr_hash_set(wc.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout, wc.q);
3735            wc.tail = write_completion_q = wc.q;
3736
3737            ka.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->keep_alive_timeout, NULL);
3738            apr_hash_set(ka.hash, &s->keep_alive_timeout,
3739                         sizeof s->keep_alive_timeout, ka.q);
3740            ka.tail = keepalive_q = ka.q;
3741        }
3742        else {
3743            /* The vhosts use any existing queue with the same timeout,
3744             * or their own queue(s) if there isn't */ view japanese
3745            io.q = apr_hash_get(io.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout);
3746            if (!io.q) {
3747                io.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->timeout, io.tail);
3748                apr_hash_set(io.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout, io.q);
3749                io.tail = io.tail->next = io.q;
3750            }
3751
3752            wc.q = apr_hash_get(wc.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout);
3753            if (!wc.q) {
3754                wc.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->timeout, wc.tail);
3755                apr_hash_set(wc.hash, &s->timeout, sizeof s->timeout, wc.q);
3756                wc.tail = wc.tail->next = wc.q;
3757            }
3758
3759            ka.q = apr_hash_get(ka.hash, &s->keep_alive_timeout,
3760                                sizeof s->keep_alive_timeout);
3761            if (!ka.q) {
3762                ka.q = TO_QUEUE_MAKE(pconf, s->keep_alive_timeout, ka.tail);
3763                apr_hash_set(ka.hash, &s->keep_alive_timeout,
3764                             sizeof s->keep_alive_timeout, ka.q);
3765                ka.tail = ka.tail->next = ka.q;
3766            }
3767        }
3768        sc->io_q = io.q;
3769        sc->wc_q = wc.q;
3770        sc->ka_q = ka.q;
3771    }
3772
3773    return OK;
3774}
3775
3776static int event_check_config(apr_pool_t *p, apr_pool_t *plog,
3777                              apr_pool_t *ptemp, server_rec *s)
3778{
3779    int startup = 0;
3780
3781    /* the reverse of pre_config, we want this only the first time around */ view japanese
3782    if (retained->mpm->module_loads == 1) {
3783        startup = 1;
3784    }
3785
3786    if (server_limit > MAX_SERVER_LIMIT) {
3787        if (startup) {
3788            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING | APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00497)
3789                         "WARNING: ServerLimit of %d exceeds compile-time "
3790                         "limit of %d servers, decreasing to %d.",
3791                         server_limit, MAX_SERVER_LIMIT, MAX_SERVER_LIMIT);
3792        } else {
